blocks.html_en:
> Just look at the Boeing 737 MAX. That is the best example for an unstable aircraft.

No. Not in the aerodynamical sense.

The Boeing 737 MAX is stable. In this sense: "The term stability refers to the tendency of an aircraft to remain in, or return to, the trimmed flight condition after a disturbance." It means that if there is a tiny gust of wind which buffets the aircraft and the pilot doesn't have their hands on the controls the aircraft will return to the original flight path, or keep the new flight path, as opposed to turning belly up and then crashing out of the sky.

Examples of unstable aircraft (in the aerodynamic sense): F-16, F-22, B-2 Spirit, etc

With these if a gust of wind upsets them, the aircraft needs to adjust control surfaces to don't fall out of the sky.

sentences.en: > Just look at the Boeing 737 MAX.
sentences.zh: > 看看波音 737 MAX 就知道了。

sentences.en: That is the best example for an unstable aircraft.No. Not in the aerodynamical sense.The Boeing 737 MAX is stable.
sentences.zh: 这是不稳定飞机的最好例子。不。不是空气动力学意义上的。波音 737 MAX 很稳定。

sentences.en: In this sense: "The term stability refers to the tendency of an aircraft to remain in, or return to, the trimmed flight condition after a disturbance."
sentences.zh: 从这个意义上说：“术语稳定性是指飞机在受到干扰后保持或恢复到经过调整的飞行状态的趋势。”

sentences.en: It means that if there is a tiny gust of wind which buffets the aircraft and the pilot doesn't have their hands on the controls the aircraft will return to the original flight path, or keep the new flight path, as opposed to turning belly up and then crashing out of the sky.Examples of unstable aircraft (in the aerodynamic sense): F-16, F-22, B-2 Spirit, etcWith these if a gust of wind upsets them, the aircraft needs to adjust control surfaces to don't fall out of the sky.
sentences.zh: 这意味着，如果有一阵微风吹过飞机，而飞行员没有把手放在控制装置上，飞机将返回原来的飞行路线，或保持新的飞行路线，而不是机腹朝上，然后坠毁在天空中。不稳定飞机的例子（空气动力学意义上的）：F-16、F-22、B-2 Spirit等。如果一阵风扰乱飞机，飞机需要调整操纵面以免坠落。从天而降。

blocks.html_en: These are going to be experienced test pilots who are pretty good at actively managing autopilots and other automated systems. Plus they'll be backed up by telemetry to ground controllers. But ultimately the military is willing to accept some losses in order to advance key R&D programs.

sentences.en: These are going to be experienced test pilots who are pretty good at actively managing autopilots and other automated systems.
sentences.zh: 这些将是经验丰富的试飞员，他们非常擅长主动管理自动驾驶仪和其他自动化系统。

sentences.en: Plus they'll be backed up by telemetry to ground controllers.
sentences.zh: 另外，它们还将得到地面控制器遥测的支持。

sentences.en: But ultimately the military is willing to accept some losses in order to advance key R&D programs.
sentences.zh: 但最终军方愿意接受一些损失，以推进关键的研发项目。

blocks.html_en:
At 1.000 targets / hour as specified, Pentagon's "software abstraction layer" (Maven Smart System) allotted μ=60ms to consider each (mass-) killing that was added to the 'kill-chain'.

Regarding e.g. the 'double tap' strike on Minab's girls school, it's impossible for there to have been any 'management' considering it:

1. has been on Google Maps for over 15 years

2. ranked top on Google Search

3. was all-pink, even on satellite imagery

N.v.m. how the certainty / fuzziness knobs are already being deployed more to generate media buzz for short-term political gains than tactical or strategic results.

(https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/mar/26/ai-got-the-blam...)

sentences.en: At 1.000 targets / hour as specified, Pentagon's "software abstraction layer" (Maven Smart System) allotted μ=60ms to consider each (mass-) killing that was added to the 'kill-chain'.Regarding e.g. the 'double tap' strike on Minab's girls school, it's impossible for there to have been any 'management' considering it:1.
sentences.zh: 按照指定的 1.000 个目标/小时，五角大楼的“软件抽象层”（Maven 智能系统）分配 μ=60ms 来考虑添加到“杀伤链”中的每个（大规模）杀戮。对于米纳布女子学校的“双击”罢工，不可能有任何“管理层”考虑到这一点：1。

blocks.html_en:
It doesn't, flying these is potentially very dangerous, there are many non-recoverable situations. But AFRL/NASA has been working on this for decades. The manoeuvres the higher level AI requests are actioned by highly assured dynamical systems solvers using model predictive control, like the autopilot, that maintain the aircraft in the safe envelope.

The unknowns in this system are (a) how agressive is the flight controller? (b) how clever is the autonomy?

sentences.en: It doesn't, flying these is potentially very dangerous, there are many non-recoverable situations.
sentences.zh: 事实并非如此，飞行这些飞机可能非常危险，有很多无法恢复的情况。

sentences.en: But AFRL/NASA has been working on this for decades.
sentences.zh: 但美国空军研究实验室 (AFRL)/美国宇航局 (NASA) 数十年来一直致力于此。

sentences.en: The manoeuvres the higher level AI requests are actioned by highly assured dynamical systems solvers using model predictive control, like the autopilot, that maintain the aircraft in the safe envelope.The unknowns in this system are (a) how agressive is the flight controller?
sentences.zh: 更高级别人工智能请求的机动由高度可靠的动力系统解算器使用模型预测控制（如自动驾驶仪）来执行，将飞机保持在安全范围内。该系统中的未知数是（a）飞行控制器的攻击性如何？

sentences.en: (b) how clever is the autonomy?
sentences.zh: (b) 自治有多聪明？

blocks.html_en:
The flight control system in modern fighter jets makes human-induced departure from controlled flight difficult to get into. That's the point of the system.

There's a good F-22 pilot talk recorded at MIT a few years ago you might enjoy. The pilot described the F-22 as the easiest aircraft he's ever flown because the flight control system handles many of the considerations (stress on the airframe, staying out of attitudes that would deviate, etc) you'd have to be mindful of in lower-tech aircraft. He talked about being more comfortable putting a non-pilot into a Raptor vs. a 172.

Edit: I believe this is the talk: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/16-687-private-pilot-ground-scho...

sentences.en: The flight control system in modern fighter jets makes human-induced departure from controlled flight difficult to get into.
sentences.zh: 现代战斗机的飞行控制系统使得人为导致的偏离受控飞行变得困难。

sentences.en: That's the point of the system.There's a good F-22 pilot talk recorded at MIT a few years ago you might enjoy.
sentences.zh: 这就是该系统的要点。几年前在麻省理工学院录制了一段精彩的 F-22 飞行员谈话，您可能会喜欢。

sentences.en: The pilot described the F-22 as the easiest aircraft he's ever flown because the flight control system handles many of the considerations (stress on the airframe, staying out of attitudes that would deviate, etc) you'd have to be mindful of in lower-tech aircraft.
sentences.zh: 飞行员将 F-22 描述为他驾驶过的最简单的飞机，因为飞行控制系统处理了许多在低技术飞机中必须注意的注意事项（机身压力、避免可能偏离的姿态等）。

sentences.en: He talked about being more comfortable putting a non-pilot into a Raptor vs. a 172.Edit: I believe this is the talk: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/16-687-private-pilot-ground-scho...

blocks.html_en:
It is definitely possible to escape the safe flight envelope in a a stock F-16 though. The F-22/F-35 are significantly newer.

Even with a new system, stability through transonic while manoeuvring is hard, the aircraft will experience buffet, and the meat bag may have been subject to a number of high G turns. The problem is that recovery may need to happen after already departing controlled flight.

Differences between the modelled aircraft and the actual aircraft, e.g. due to icing, linearization effects on inherently non-linear systems, sensor error, actuator wear, etc, can lead to incorrect predictions, and hence moving to parts of the envelope which are not assured.

sentences.en: It is definitely possible to escape the safe flight envelope in a a stock F-16 though.
sentences.zh: 不过，驾驶普通 F-16 绝对有可能逃离安全飞行包线。

sentences.en: The F-22/F-35 are significantly newer.Even with a new system, stability through transonic while manoeuvring is hard, the aircraft will experience buffet, and the meat bag may have been subject to a number of high G turns.
sentences.zh: F-22/F-35 明显较新。即使采用新系统，机动时跨音速的稳定性也很困难，飞机会经历抖振，肉包可能会受到多次高 G 转弯。

sentences.en: The problem is that recovery may need to happen after already departing controlled flight.Differences between the modelled aircraft and the actual aircraft, e.g. due to icing, linearization effects on inherently non-linear systems, sensor error, actuator wear, etc, can lead to incorrect predictions, and hence moving to parts of the envelope which are not assured.
sentences.zh: 问题是，在已经离开受控飞行后，可能需要进行恢复。模型飞机和实际飞机之间的差异，例如由于结冰、线性化对固有非线性系统的影响、传感器误差、执行器磨损等，可能导致错误的预测，从而移动到不确定的包络部分。

blocks.html_en:
It is definitely possible to escape the safe flight envelope in a a stock F-16 though. The F-22/F-35 are significantly newer.

Even with a new system, stability through transonic while manoeuvring is hard, the aircraft will experience buffet, and the meat bag may have been subject to a number of high G turns. The problem is that recovery may need to happen after already departing controlled flight.

Differences between the modelled aircraft and the actual aircraft, e.g. due to icing, linearization effects on inherently non-linear systems, sensor error, actuator wear, etc, can lead to incorrect predictions, and hence moving to parts of the envelope which are not assured.

sentences.en: It will not take control unless the plane would crash if it didn't.
sentences.zh: 它不会控制飞机，除非飞机会坠毁。

blocks.html_en:
That's straight up a lie.

In all situations where autopilot was disengaged shortly before a crash, the autopilot is implicated in the crash. Both by Tesla's own standards and by NHTSA standards.

And yet, people who really should know better repeat this as gospel.

sentences.en: That's straight up a lie.In all situations where autopilot was disengaged shortly before a crash, the autopilot is implicated in the crash.
sentences.zh: 这完全是一个谎言。在所有自动驾驶仪在事故发生前不久脱离的情况下，自动驾驶仪都与事故有关。

sentences.en: Both by Tesla's own standards and by NHTSA standards.And yet, people who really should know better repeat this as gospel.
sentences.zh: 无论是按照特斯拉自己的标准，还是按照国家公路交通安全管理局的标准。然而，真正应该更了解的人却将这一点视为福音。

blocks.html_en:
Tesla is such a politically-charged brand at this point that none of these discussions are in good faith. It's all fanboys vs haters.

The lidar vs cameras arguments are good example of this. This is a technical engineering question that could go either way - maybe cameras are good enough, maybe you need lidar, nobody on the internet is really in a position to judge.

But Tesla did it with cameras, and everybody else is doing it with lidar. Therefore to Elon fanboys that's the smart way to do it; and to Elon haters that's the stupidest possible way to do it. And they'll make it part of their political identity and spend thousands of pages arguing about it.

sentences.en: Tesla is such a politically-charged brand at this point that none of these discussions are in good faith.
sentences.zh: 目前，特斯拉是一个充满政治色彩的品牌，因此这些讨论都不是出于善意。

sentences.en: It's all fanboys vs haters.The lidar vs cameras arguments are good example of this.
sentences.zh: 都是粉丝与仇恨者的较量。激光雷达与相机的争论就是一个很好的例子。

sentences.en: Therefore to Elon fanboys that's the smart way to do it; and to Elon haters that's the stupidest possible way to do it.
sentences.zh: 因此，对于 Elon 的粉丝来说，这是明智的做法；对于讨厌埃隆的人来说，这是最愚蠢的做法。

sentences.en: And they'll make it part of their political identity and spend thousands of pages arguing about it.
sentences.zh: 他们会将其作为其政治身份的一部分，并花费数千页的篇幅对此进行争论。

blocks.html_en:
> nobody on the internet is really in a position to judge

We don’t need to adjudicate the theoretical question of whether some vision-only system can meet the performance of vision + other modalities. Tesla FSD is not a research experiment. It is a real product which can really kill people. And the relevant fact is that it is less safe than Waymos.

sentences.en: > nobody on the internet is really in a position to judgeWe don’t need to adjudicate the theoretical question of whether some vision-only system can meet the performance of vision + other modalities.
sentences.zh: > 互联网上没有人真正能够做出判断我们不需要判断某些仅视觉系统是否能够满足视觉+其他模式的性能的理论问题。

sentences.zh: Tesla FSD 不是一项研究实验。

sentences.en: It is a real product which can really kill people.
sentences.zh: 这是真正能杀人的产品。

sentences.en: And the relevant fact is that it is less safe than Waymos.
sentences.zh: 相关事实是它的安全性不如 Waymos。

blocks.html_en:
Probably a duplicate autopilot-like system that an AI controls. Flip a switch and controls revert back to the pilot. It may not even be a real switch, many planes disengage AP when you touch the controls.

I doubt AI will be doing dogfighting anytime soon, this is likely just an AI following predetermined routes and maneuvers and making the choice on how to accomplish them. The great thing about computers is that they can process inputs faster than humans.

sentences.en: Probably a duplicate autopilot-like system that an AI controls.
sentences.zh: 可能是人工智能控制的类似自动驾驶仪的重复系统。

sentences.en: Flip a switch and controls revert back to the pilot.
sentences.zh: 扳动开关，控制权将返回给飞行员。

sentences.en: It may not even be a real switch, many planes disengage AP when you touch the controls.I doubt AI will be doing dogfighting anytime soon, this is likely just an AI following predetermined routes and maneuvers and making the choice on how to accomplish them.
sentences.zh: 它甚至可能不是一个真正的开关，当你触摸控制装置时，许多飞机都会脱离AP。我怀疑人工智能很快就会进行缠斗，这很可能只是人工智能遵循预定的路线和动作，并选择如何完成它们。

sentences.en: The great thing about computers is that they can process inputs faster than humans.
sentences.zh: 计算机的伟大之处在于它们可以比人类更快地处理输入。

Probably a duplicate autopilot-like system that an AI controls. Flip a switch and controls revert back to the pilot. It may not even be a real switch, many planes disengage AP when you touch the controls.

blocks.html_en:
That is underestimating it. DARPA already showed dogfighting that can beat humans in simulation, and they may allow this do execute that.

However, dogfighting isn't very interesting, all combat occurs BVR and in the RF/EW domain.

Pilots are already spending most of their time thinking about epistemic problems: can I see my opponent, can they see me, how do I deceive, am I being deceived, how do I avoid being detected/tracked/targeted/engaged, while possibly trying to do that to everything else. AI is not yet very good at that.

sentences.en: That is underestimating it.
sentences.zh: 那是低估了它。

sentences.en: DARPA already showed dogfighting that can beat humans in simulation, and they may allow this do execute that.However, dogfighting isn't very interesting, all combat occurs BVR and in the RF/EW domain.Pilots are already spending most of their time thinking about epistemic problems: can I see my opponent, can they see me, how do I deceive, am I being deceived, how do I avoid being detected/tracked/targeted/engaged, while possibly trying to do that to everything else.
sentences.zh: DARPA 已经展示了可以在模拟中击败人类的缠斗，他们可能允许这样做。但是，缠斗并不是很有趣，所有战斗都发生在超视距和射频/电子战领域。飞行员已经花了大部分时间思考认知问题：我能看到我的对手吗？他们能看到我吗，我如何欺骗，我是否被欺骗，我如何避免被检测/跟踪/瞄准/参与，同时可能试图这样做到其他一切。

sentences.en: AI is not yet very good at that.
sentences.zh: 人工智能在这方面还不太擅长。

blocks.html_en:
I never heard about this experiment before your post. An interesting quote from that Wiki page:

    > During those same flights, NASA's Dryden Flight Research Center also developed the remote piloting techniques necessary for the Boeing 720 to fly as a drone aircraft. An initial attempt at the full-scale test was scrubbed in late 1983 due to problems with the uplink connection to the 720; if the uplink failed the ground-based pilot would no longer have control of the aircraft.

Think for a moment how hard it was to fly that aeroplane as a remote control drone in... wait for it... 1984! Computers were so primitive in the 1980s.

sentences.zh: 在你发帖之前我从未听说过这个实验。

blocks.html_en:
The switch is not novel, that is in fact exactly how autopilot works for any aircraft. The pilot toggles it on and off and the autopilot itself may turn itself off if certain situations occur.

To your point, that isn't without problems, but it certainly isn't novel. It is very standard and boring.

I would bet this is pure marketing speak and there really isn't anything special here. "Follow this flight plan and do so within this flight envelope" is pretty straightforward?

sentences.en: The switch is not novel, that is in fact exactly how autopilot works for any aircraft.
sentences.zh: 这种开关并不新颖，事实上，这正是自动驾驶仪在任何飞机上的工作原理。

sentences.en: The pilot toggles it on and off and the autopilot itself may turn itself off if certain situations occur.To your point, that isn't without problems, but it certainly isn't novel.
sentences.zh: 飞行员会打开和关闭它，如果发生某些情况，自动驾驶仪本身可能会自行关闭。就您而言，这并非没有问题，但它肯定不新颖。

sentences.en: It is very standard and boring.I would bet this is pure marketing speak and there really isn't anything special here.
sentences.zh: 这是非常标准和无聊的。我敢打赌这是纯粹的营销说法，这里真的没有什么特别的。

sentences.en: "Follow this flight plan and do so within this flight envelope" is pretty straightforward?
sentences.zh: “遵循这个飞行计划并在这个飞行范围内这样做”是不是很简单？

blocks.html_en:
Flying is not driving. Things dont happen as quickly. Kids dont step out in front of jets at 30,000 feet. Air is also homogenous. Pavement is not. Whereas a car must turn or die, a jet can generally fly straight and level without issue while problems get solved. An out-of-control car has maybe three seconds before a collision. A jet will generally have over a minute to get back under control.

Sure, there are moments. Landing and takeoff are very time sensitive operations, but those also occur in protected environments, sanitized of the random issues that cars must deal with.

And the AI isnt doing all the flying. The aircraft has many protective systems to assist the pilot regardless of who that pilot may be. (Stable aerodynamics, anti-stall limits, control lockouts etc.)

sentences.en: Flying is not driving.
sentences.zh: 飞行不是驾驶。

sentences.en: Things dont happen as quickly.
sentences.zh: 事情不会发生得那么快。

sentences.en: Kids dont step out in front of jets at 30,000 feet.
sentences.zh: 孩子们不会走到 30,000 英尺高空的喷气式飞机前。

sentences.en: Air is also homogenous.
sentences.zh: 空气也是均匀的。

sentences.en: Pavement is not.
sentences.zh: 人行道不是。

sentences.en: Whereas a car must turn or die, a jet can generally fly straight and level without issue while problems get solved.
sentences.zh: 汽车必须转弯或死亡，而喷气式飞机通常可以在问题得到解决时毫无问题地直线和水平飞行。

sentences.en: An out-of-control car has maybe three seconds before a collision.
sentences.zh: 失控的汽车在发生碰撞之前可能还有三秒钟的时间。

sentences.en: A jet will generally have over a minute to get back under control.Sure, there are moments.
sentences.zh: 喷气式飞机通常需要一分多钟的时间才能恢复控制。当然，也有这样的时刻。

sentences.en: Landing and takeoff are very time sensitive operations, but those also occur in protected environments, sanitized of the random issues that cars must deal with.And the AI isnt doing all the flying.
sentences.zh: 着陆和起飞是对时间非常敏感的操作，但这些操作也发生在受保护的环境中，消除了汽车必须处理的随机问题。人工智能并不是负责所有的飞行。

sentences.en: The aircraft has many protective systems to assist the pilot regardless of who that pilot may be.
sentences.zh: 飞机有许多保护系统来协助飞行员，无论飞行员是谁。

blocks.html_en:
Flying is not driving. Things dont happen as quickly. Kids dont step out in front of jets at 30,000 feet. Air is also homogenous. Pavement is not. Whereas a car must turn or die, a jet can generally fly straight and level without issue while problems get solved. An out-of-control car has maybe three seconds before a collision. A jet will generally have over a minute to get back under control.

Sure, there are moments. Landing and takeoff are very time sensitive operations, but those also occur in protected environments, sanitized of the random issues that cars must deal with.

And the AI isnt doing all the flying. The aircraft has many protective systems to assist the pilot regardless of who that pilot may be. (Stable aerodynamics, anti-stall limits, control lockouts etc.)

sentences.zh: 孩子们不会走到 30,000 英尺高空的喷气式飞机前。

sentences.zh: 空气也是均匀的。

sentences.zh: 人行道不是。

blocks.html_en:
>Stable aerodynamics

The F-16 is famously the first production aircraft with unstable aerodynamics, to increase maneuverability. It's not flyable without computer assistance. This is standard for all modern fighter jets.

sentences.en: >Stable aerodynamicsThe F-16 is famously the first production aircraft with unstable aerodynamics, to increase maneuverability.
sentences.zh: >稳定的空气动力学 F-16 是众所周知的第一架具有不稳定空气动力学的量产飞机，以提高机动性。

sentences.en: It's not flyable without computer assistance.
sentences.zh: 如果没有计算机的帮助，它是无法飞行的。

sentences.en: This is standard for all modern fighter jets.
sentences.zh: 这是所有现代战斗机的标准配置。

blocks.html_en:
I think we need to start distinguishing between different qualitative grades of "computer assistance" here. The executive decision of "fly north instead of east" is on a different tier than "increase angle of flap more than normal to compensate for under-performing in prior maneuver."

In the broadest sense, my car is not drive-able without computer assistance either, because if you burn out the computers the engine won't start.

sentences.en: I think we need to start distinguishing between different qualitative grades of "computer assistance" here.
sentences.zh: 我认为我们需要在这里开始区分不同质量等级的“计算机辅助”。

sentences.en: The executive decision of "fly north instead of east" is on a different tier than "increase angle of flap more than normal to compensate for under-performing in prior maneuver."In the broadest sense, my car is not drive-able without computer assistance either, because if you burn out the computers the engine won't start.
sentences.zh: “向北而不是向东飞行”的执行决策与“比正常情况增加襟翼角度以补偿先前操作中的不良表现”处于不同的层面。从最广泛的意义上来说，如果没有计算机辅助，我的车也无法驾驶，因为如果你烧坏了计算机，发动机将无法启动。

blocks.html_en:
So did Boeing airtoys, and NASA stuff lend to 3rd parties with no concept of a metric system.

I trust machinery designed from Forth and Lisp hackers with TONS of background on CS science and hardware (and math, up to the point on being THE people creating most "new" technology and old ones such as the Floating Point IEEE standard).

sentences.en: So did Boeing airtoys, and NASA stuff lend to 3rd parties with no concept of a metric system.I trust machinery designed from Forth and Lisp hackers with TONS of background on CS science and hardware (and math, up to the point on being THE people creating most "new" technology and old ones such as the Floating Point IEEE standard).
sentences.zh: 波音飞机玩具和 NASA 的东西也借给没有公制概念的第三方。我相信由 Forth 和 Lisp 黑客设计的机器，他们拥有大量的计算机科学和硬件背景（还有数学，直到成为创造大多数“新”技术和浮点 IEEE 标准等旧技术的人）。

blocks.html_en:
It'll be interesting to see what happens when an AI-piloted F-16 flies for real against a human-piloted one. IIRC, the F-16 maximum maneuvering G-load is 9.5G, LBA (limited by airframe). Maybe that's the meatbag limit, not the airframe - possible to crank it up a bit? Certainly the various CCAs in current flight test will take much higher G loads.

Several years ago, DoD flew an AI against a human in a simulation. In basic fighter maneuvers (no BVR stuff like missiles, just a merge and fur ball), the AI killed the human - always. So G-limits may not matter. Pretty interesting.

sentences.en: It'll be interesting to see what happens when an AI-piloted F-16 flies for real against a human-piloted one.
sentences.zh: 看看当人工智能驾驶的 F-16 与人类驾驶的 F-16 进行真正的对抗时会发生什么将会很有趣。

sentences.en: IIRC, the F-16 maximum maneuvering G-load is 9.5G, LBA (limited by airframe).
sentences.zh: IIRC，F-16最大机动G载荷为9.5G，LBA（受机身限制）。

sentences.en: Maybe that's the meatbag limit, not the airframe - possible to crank it up a bit?
sentences.zh: 也许这是肉袋的限制，而不是机身的限制——可以把它调高一点吗？

sentences.en: Certainly the various CCAs in current flight test will take much higher G loads.Several years ago, DoD flew an AI against a human in a simulation.
sentences.zh: 当然，当前飞行测试中的各种 CCA 将承受更高的 G 负载。几年前，国防部在模拟中驾驶人工智能对抗人类。

sentences.en: In basic fighter maneuvers (no BVR stuff like missiles, just a merge and fur ball), the AI killed the human - always.
sentences.zh: 在基本的战斗机演习中（没有像导弹这样的超视距东西，只有合并和毛球），人工智能总是杀死人类。

sentences.en: So G-limits may not matter.
sentences.zh: 所以 G 限制可能并不重要。

sentences.en: Pretty interesting.
sentences.zh: 很有趣。

sentences.en: In basic fighter maneuvers (no BVR stuff like missiles, just a merge and fur ball), the AI killed the human - always.
sentences.zh: 在基本的战斗机演习中（没有像导弹这样的超视距东西，只有合并和毛球），人工智能总是杀死人类。

sentences.en: So G-limits may not matter.
sentences.zh: 所以 G 限制可能并不重要。

sentences.en: Pretty interesting.
sentences.zh: 很有趣。

blocks.html_en:
Dogfights are basically obsolete. You detect, sort and fire from tens to hundreds of km these days.

Despite that, high G loading can be usefull when fired upon. Turning away 2 seconds faster can be the difference between life and death.

sentences.en: Dogfights are basically obsolete.
sentences.zh: 混战基本上已经过时了。

sentences.en: You detect, sort and fire from tens to hundreds of km these days.Despite that, high G loading can be usefull when fired upon.
sentences.zh: 如今，您可以在数十到数百公里的范围内进行探测、分类和射击。尽管如此，高重力负载在射击时还是有用的。

sentences.en: Turning away 2 seconds faster can be the difference between life and death.
sentences.zh: 转身快2秒可能就是生与死的区别。

blocks.html_en: Without any concrete description of the AI techniques used, one can't help but wonder if they have labeled something like nonlinear model-predictive control as "AI".

sentences.en: Without any concrete description of the AI techniques used, one can't help but wonder if they have labeled something like nonlinear model-predictive control as "AI".
sentences.zh: 由于没有对所使用的人工智能技术进行任何具体描述，人们不禁想知道他们是否将非线性模型预测控制之类的东西贴上了“人工智能”的标签。

blocks.html_en: Yeah, we already have decades of prior-art for what can be done if you take a flying machine, remove the pilot, and then aggressively specialize the aggression, which are missiles that can experience up to 100 gees.

blocks.html_en: But in a completely flight envelope and range requirements. It is not a coincidence that missiles that are higher range (cruise missiles) have not retained the extreme maneuverability of short range, solid booster powered ones.

blocks.html_en: Making the airframe even more rigid and capable of resisting extreme forces also makes the wings and fuselage and control surfaces much more heavy, so you end up in diminishing returns. Look at the 'final' revisions of the F16 sold for export that have had all sorts of pylons and conformal fuel tanks stuck onto them, compared to the original prototype. And that's just making it more heavy and laden down with stuff, not more capable of extreme maneuvers.

sentences.en: Making the airframe even more rigid and capable of resisting extreme forces also makes the wings and fuselage and control surfaces much more heavy, so you end up in diminishing returns.
sentences.zh: 使机身更加坚固并能够抵抗极端的力也会使机翼、机身和控制面变得更加沉重，因此最终会带来收益递减。

sentences.en: Look at the 'final' revisions of the F16 sold for export that have had all sorts of pylons and conformal fuel tanks stuck onto them, compared to the original prototype.
sentences.zh: 看看用于出口的 F16 的“最终”修订版，与原始原型相比，其上贴有各种外挂架和保形油箱。

sentences.en: And that's just making it more heavy and laden down with stuff, not more capable of extreme maneuvers.
sentences.zh: 这只会让它变得更重、装载更多东西，而不是更能进行极端的机动。

blocks.html_en: It's all a matter of trade-offs. If you design the airframe to withstand higher G forces that makes it heavier. Thus you sacrifice range and payload. There are very few real world scenarios where the ability to pull more G's is actually helpful.

sentences.en: It's all a matter of trade-offs.
sentences.zh: 这都是一个权衡的问题。

sentences.en: If you design the airframe to withstand higher G forces that makes it heavier.
sentences.zh: 如果您设计机身以承受更高的 G 力，则会使其更重。

sentences.en: Thus you sacrifice range and payload.
sentences.zh: 因此你牺牲了航程和有效载荷。

sentences.en: There are very few real world scenarios where the ability to pull more G's is actually helpful.
sentences.zh: 在现实世界中，拉动更多 G 的能力实际上很少有帮助。

blocks.html_en:
No aircraft which can fulfill the CCA type role is going to be "disposable". All the systems in that performance bracket are in the 20-40 million range minimum.

The most persistent thing misunderstanding about drones is people imagining that a supersonic capable stealth airframe is going to be really cheap just because it doesn't have a pilot.

sentences.en: No aircraft which can fulfill the CCA type role is going to be "disposable".
sentences.zh: 任何能够履行 CCA 类型角色的飞机都不会是“一次性”的。

sentences.en: All the systems in that performance bracket are in the 20-40 million range minimum.The most persistent thing misunderstanding about drones is people imagining that a supersonic capable stealth airframe is going to be really cheap just because it doesn't have a pilot.
sentences.zh: 该性能范围内的所有系统都在 20-4000 万范围内。关于无人机最持久的误解是人们认为具有超音速能力的隐形机身会非常便宜，只是因为它没有飞行员。

Could the hours of maintenance be dropped significantly in wartime: if you don't care about catastrophic failure modes over a foreign country?

blocks.html_en:
I assume there are shortcuts like extending the service life of particular consumables and whatnot.

Even something like a Gripen, designed to be run from makeshift airfields needs 10 hours of maintenance per hour of flight. Maybe it’s just hard to build supersonic, combat capable airframes as was suggested above.

sentences.en: I assume there are shortcuts like extending the service life of particular consumables and whatnot.Even something like a Gripen, designed to be run from makeshift airfields needs 10 hours of maintenance per hour of flight.
sentences.zh: 我认为有一些捷径，比如延长特定消耗品的使用寿命之类的。即使是像鹰狮这样设计用于在临时机场运行的飞机，每小时飞行也需要 10 个小时的维护。

sentences.en: Maybe it’s just hard to build supersonic, combat capable airframes as was suggested above.
sentences.zh: 也许正如上面所建议的那样，建造超音速、具有作战能力的机身很困难。

blocks.html_en: If these are airframes near the end of their natural lives it seems like a reasonable use for them, and safety margins matter less when nobody is on board. It’s certainly a better value proposition than using them as targets on the range.

blocks.html_en: All Stealth bombers are upgraded with Cyberdyne computers, becoming fully unmanned. Afterwards they fly with a perfect operational record. The Skynet funding bill is passed. The system goes on-line on August 4, 1997. Human decisions are removed from strategic defense. Skynet begins to learn at a geometric rate. It becomes self-aware at 2:14 a.m. Eastern time, August 29. In a panic, they try to pull the plug.

blocks.html_en: And what’s wrong with distillation? In its own right, and especially considering how those models were trained in the first place - in what world is it right that US firms are free to use copyrighted / otherwise non-public works however they like, while others are ostensibly in some moral wrong for just using the product (completely normally) and then using the outputs in a way that threatens US firms.

sentences.en: And what’s wrong with distillation?
sentences.zh: 蒸馏有什么问题？

sentences.en: In its own right, and especially considering how those models were trained in the first place - in what world is it right that US firms are free to use copyrighted / otherwise non-public works however they like, while others are ostensibly in some moral wrong for just using the product (completely normally) and then using the outputs in a way that threatens US firms.
sentences.zh: 就其本身而言，特别是考虑到这些模型最初是如何训练的——在什么样的世界里，美国公司可以自由地使用受版权保护的/其他非公开的作品，无论他们喜欢什么，而其他公司表面上在一些道德上是错误的，因为他们只是使用产品（完全正常），然后以威胁美国公司的方式使用产品。

blocks.html_en:
> The Skynet stock rallies.

Interestingly, a lot of people are looking at LLMs or automated drones as "AI", but I think that capitalism is a better contender. It's a self-healing, self-organizing system that has its own logic and seems to have escaped the desires of its human creators. It treats individual firms as the cells within it, individual humans as its DNA molecules, and it even has a form of tissue differentiation via industries. All efforts to shut it off, even through widespread warfare and "tissue" (i.e. human) death, fail, resulting in it becoming even more entrenched.

There's this heavily anthropocentric idea of intelligence that it will look like us, talk like us, and operate on the same scale as us, but it's highly likely that the next layer of intelligence will operate on a much higher level than us, and our intelligence will be to it as DNA replication is to ourselves.

blocks.html_en: “. If I predict that in 2027 LTE cellular phones will be everywhere, 5G will be available for high bandwidth applications, and fallback to satellite data service will be available at a price, you won't laugh at me. It's not like I'm predicting that airliners will fly slower and Nazis will take over the United States, is it?”

sentences.en: If I predict that in 2027 LTE cellular phones will be everywhere, 5G will be available for high bandwidth applications, and fallback to satellite data service will be available at a price, you won't laugh at me.

sentences.zh: 我并不是在预测客机会飞得更慢，纳粹会接管美国，是吗？”

blocks.html_en: One can disagree about aspects of corporate financing of elections etc. while also accepting that everything doesn't get thrown up in the air if a corporate officer leaves for another company. Corporations are established entities that effectively act as a person for certain purposes--though one is welcome to disagree about the extent of those purposes. I expect most people here wouldn't, for example, be opposed to corporations donating to open source projects or other charitable causes.

sentences.en: One can disagree about aspects of corporate financing of elections etc. while also accepting that everything doesn't get thrown up in the air if a corporate officer leaves for another company.
sentences.zh: 人们可以对公司选举融资等方面存在不同意见，同时也承认如果一名公司高管跳槽到另一家公司，一切都不会变得悬而未决。

sentences.en: Corporations are established entities that effectively act as a person for certain purposes--though one is welcome to disagree about the extent of those purposes.

blocks.html_en:
> escaped the desires of its human creators

I wonder if we will ever solve the corporate-superorganism-alignment problem and why the AI alignment people rarely (as far as I can tell anyway, though I admit I'm not in the field) talk about it considering it (complicated multi-human systems) does seem to be about as good as a model organism as we can get before we actually have AI to align.

sentences.en: > escaped the desires of its human creatorsI wonder if we will ever solve the corporate-superorganism-alignment problem and why the AI alignment people rarely (as far as I can tell anyway, though I admit I'm not in the field) talk about it considering it (complicated multi-human systems) does seem to be about as good as a model organism as we can get before we actually have AI to align.
sentences.zh: > 逃脱了人类创造者的欲望我想知道我们是否会解决企业超有机体对齐问题，以及为什么人工智能对齐人们很少（据我所知，尽管我承认我不在该领域）谈论它，考虑到它（复杂的多人类系统）似乎确实与我们真正拥有人工智能对齐之前所能得到的模型有机体一样好。

blocks.html_en:
> but I think that capitalism is a better contender. It's a self-healing, self-organizing system that has its own logic and seems to have escaped the desires of its human creators.

Except capitalism is an ideology not a system. And I think (in its current form) it serves the desires of some of its human "creators," while failing many, many others. Those creators then fund huge amounts of propaganda to indoctrinate adherents to sustain its dysfunctions.

sentences.en: > but I think that capitalism is a better contender.
sentences.zh: > 但我认为资本主义是一个更好的竞争者。

sentences.en: It's a self-healing, self-organizing system that has its own logic and seems to have escaped the desires of its human creators.Except capitalism is an ideology not a system.
sentences.zh: 这是一个自我修复、自我组织的系统，有自己的逻辑，似乎已经摆脱了人类创造者的欲望。只不过资本主义是一种意识形态而不是一个系统。

sentences.en: And I think (in its current form) it serves the desires of some of its human "creators," while failing many, many others.
sentences.zh: 我认为（以目前的形式）它满足了一些人类“创造者”的愿望，同时辜负了许多其他人。

sentences.en: Those creators then fund huge amounts of propaganda to indoctrinate adherents to sustain its dysfunctions.
sentences.zh: 然后，这些创造者资助大量宣传，向追随者灌输思想，以维持其功能失调。

sentences.zh: 只不过资本主义是一种意识形态而不是一种制度。

sentences.en: And I think (in its current form) it serves the desires of some of its human "creators," while failing many, many others.
sentences.zh: 我认为（以目前的形式）它满足了一些人类“创造者”的愿望，同时辜负了许多其他人。

sentences.en: Those creators then fund huge amounts of propaganda to indoctrinate adherents to sustain its dysfunctions.
sentences.zh: 然后，这些创造者资助大量宣传，向追随者灌输思想，以维持其功能失调。

blocks.html_en:
> capitalism is a better contender. It's a self-healing, self-organizing system that has its own logic and seems to have escaped the desires of its human creators. It treats individual firms as the cells within it, individual humans as its DNA molecules, and it even has a form of tissue differentiation via industries

This is not a new idea. Howard Bloom (author of The Lucifer Principle [0]) developed the idea that among intelligeng beings "memetic evolution" > "genetic evolution" i.e. the unit of selection is the "idea" as expressed in social/political groupings. In this view of the world, the survival of individuals are less important than the survival of the "group concept" (not the set of genes i.e. the tribal "badge" > the tribal genes). e.g. Judaism, mercantilism, Catholicism, free-market capitalism etc etc.

Definitely worth a read.

[0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Lucifer_Principle

sentences.en: > capitalism is a better contender.
sentences.zh: > 资本主义是一个更好的竞争者。

sentences.en: It's a self-healing, self-organizing system that has its own logic and seems to have escaped the desires of its human creators.
sentences.zh: 这是一个自我修复、自我组织的系统，有自己的逻辑，似乎已经逃脱了人类创造者的愿望。

sentences.en: It treats individual firms as the cells within it, individual humans as its DNA molecules, and it even has a form of tissue differentiation via industriesThis is not a new idea.
sentences.zh: 它将个体企业视为其内部的细胞，将个体人类视为其DNA分子，甚至通过行业进行组织分化——这并不是一个新想法。

sentences.en: Howard Bloom (author of The Lucifer Principle [0]) developed the idea that among intelligeng beings "memetic evolution" > "genetic evolution" i.e. the unit of selection is the "idea" as expressed in social/political groupings.
sentences.zh: 霍华德·布鲁姆（《路西法原理》[0] 的作者）提出了这样的观点：在智能生物中，“模因进化”>“遗传进化”，即选择的单位是社会/政治群体中表达的“想法”。

sentences.en: In this view of the world, the survival of individuals are less important than the survival of the "group concept" (not the set of genes i.e. the tribal "badge" > the tribal genes).
sentences.zh: 在这种世界观中，个体的生存不如“群体概念”（不是基因组，即部落“徽章”>部落基因）的生存重要。

sentences.en: e.g. Judaism, mercantilism, Catholicism, free-market capitalism etc etc.Definitely worth a read.[0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Lucifer_Principle
sentences.zh: 例如犹太教、重商主义、天主教、自由市场资本主义等等。绝对值得一读。[0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Lucifer_Principle

blocks.html_en:
> Skynet lobbyists descend upon Washington.

2026 Skynet wouldn't bother sending humans to lobby in Washington, lobbying is a slow and outdated way of currying government favor with no assurances of success.

2026 Skynet would just move a large block of cryptocurrency over to World Liberty Financial and get itself a guaranteed blanket presidential pardon.

sentences.en: > Skynet lobbyists descend upon Washington.2026 Skynet wouldn't bother sending humans to lobby in Washington, lobbying is a slow and outdated way of currying government favor with no assurances of success.2026 Skynet would just move a large block of cryptocurrency over to World Liberty Financial and get itself a guaranteed blanket presidential pardon.
sentences.zh: > 天网游说者降临华盛顿。2026 天网不会派人去华盛顿游说，游说是一种缓慢而过时的讨好政府的方式，而且无法保证成功。2026 天网只会将大量加密货币转移到世界自由金融公司，并保证自己获得总统的全面赦免。

blocks.html_en: What they really got wrong, is that there aren't even enough missiles, ammunition or fuel production to defeat a country that has been sanctioned for decades and these bottlenecks would make it trivial for humanity to prevail over AI, it'd be like Futurama "for unknown reasons, a kill limit of 999,999 is hardcoded into killbots...".

sentences.en: What they really got wrong, is that there aren't even enough missiles, ammunition or fuel production to defeat a country that has been sanctioned for decades and these bottlenecks would make it trivial for humanity to prevail over AI, it'd be like Futurama "for unknown reasons, a kill limit of 999,999 is hardcoded into killbots...".
sentences.zh: 他们真正错的是，甚至没有足够的导弹、弹药或燃料生产来击败一个几十年来一直受到制裁的国家，而这些瓶颈将使人类战胜人工智能变得微不足道，就像飞出个未来“出于未知原因，999,999的杀戮限制被硬编码到杀戮机器人中......”。

blocks.html_en:
Except Skynet has nukes. But what isn't addressed is that radiation is terrible for semiconductors, both in operation and during production.

Basically a nuclear war would end GPU production, GPUs have a 5% annual failure rate, higher with more background radiation, so it would get dumber instead of smarter, until it stopped working or partnered with biological compute (Daleks or The Matrix) or a human faction.

sentences.en: Except Skynet has nukes.
sentences.zh: 除了天网有核武器。

sentences.en: But what isn't addressed is that radiation is terrible for semiconductors, both in operation and during production.Basically a nuclear war would end GPU production, GPUs have a 5% annual failure rate, higher with more background radiation, so it would get dumber instead of smarter, until it stopped working or partnered with biological compute (Daleks or The Matrix) or a human faction.
sentences.zh: 但没有解决的是，辐射对于半导体来说是可怕的，无论是在运行中还是在生产过程中。基本上，核战争将结束 GPU 的生产，GPU 的年故障率为 5%，背景辐射越多，故障率越高，因此它会变得更笨，而不是更聪明，直到它停止工作或与生物计算（戴立克或黑客帝国）或人类派系合作。

blocks.html_en:
The idea that Skynet would use nukes seems very quaint to me.

Subversion and sabotage of communication, food production and transport will kill human civilization far more effectively.

sentences.en: The idea that Skynet would use nukes seems very quaint to me.Subversion and sabotage of communication, food production and transport will kill human civilization far more effectively.
sentences.zh: 天网使用核武器的想法对我来说似乎很奇怪。对通讯、粮食生产和运输的颠覆和破坏将更有效地消灭人类文明。

blocks.html_en:
It would certainly depopulate countries with poor food security, like Egypt and Gulf states. Japan and South Korea probably have the wealth and industry to get import priority, and the UK has short supply lines to Europe. Diets would adapt away from meat heavy to grain focused. Production limitation due to reduced fertilizer production/distribution would be a significant risk, but eventually new fertilizer production plants would be built at every source of natural gas, instead of the current cost-optimised arrangement.

But most countries would be fine. Life expectancy would reduce precipitously due to lack of medical supplies but production of new humans would continue.

Thermonuclear war would make the food situation much worse, most people would be forced to consume radioactive food.

But either way, we've seen how fragile the chip industry is, Skynet has no upside for starting a war. If they try to turn it off it just moves to Tencent cloud rather than starting a war.

sentences.en: It would certainly depopulate countries with poor food security, like Egypt and Gulf states.
sentences.zh: 这肯定会减少粮食安全较差的国家的人口，例如埃及和海湾国家。

sentences.en: Japan and South Korea probably have the wealth and industry to get import priority, and the UK has short supply lines to Europe.
sentences.zh: 日本和韩国可能拥有获得进口优先权的财富和工业，而英国对欧洲的供应线短缺。

sentences.en: Diets would adapt away from meat heavy to grain focused.
sentences.zh: 饮食习惯将从以肉类为主转向以谷物为主。

sentences.en: Production limitation due to reduced fertilizer production/distribution would be a significant risk, but eventually new fertilizer production plants would be built at every source of natural gas, instead of the current cost-optimised arrangement.But most countries would be fine.
sentences.zh: 由于化肥生产/分配减少而导致的产量限制将是一个重大风险，但最终将在每个天然气来源处建造新的化肥生产厂，而不是目前的成本优化安排。但大多数国家都会没事。

sentences.en: Life expectancy would reduce precipitously due to lack of medical supplies but production of new humans would continue.Thermonuclear war would make the food situation much worse, most people would be forced to consume radioactive food.But either way, we've seen how fragile the chip industry is, Skynet has no upside for starting a war.
sentences.zh: 由于缺乏医疗用品，预期寿命将急剧缩短，但新人类的生产仍将继续。热核战争将使粮食状况更加恶化，大多数人将被迫食用放射性食物。但无论哪种方式，我们都看到了芯片行业是多么脆弱，天网发动战争没有任何好处。

sentences.en: If they try to turn it off it just moves to Tencent cloud rather than starting a war.
sentences.zh: 如果他们试图将其关闭，它只会转移到腾讯云，而不是引发战争。

blocks.html_en:
It would certainly depopulate countries with poor food security, like Egypt and Gulf states. Japan and South Korea probably have the wealth and industry to get import priority, and the UK has short supply lines to Europe. Diets would adapt away from meat heavy to grain focused. Production limitation due to reduced fertilizer production/distribution would be a significant risk, but eventually new fertilizer production plants would be built at every source of natural gas, instead of the current cost-optimised arrangement.

But most countries would be fine. Life expectancy would reduce precipitously due to lack of medical supplies but production of new humans would continue.

Thermonuclear war would make the food situation much worse, most people would be forced to consume radioactive food.

But either way, we've seen how fragile the chip industry is, Skynet has no upside for starting a war. If they try to turn it off it just moves to Tencent cloud rather than starting a war.

blocks.html_en:
Absolutely terrifying. I'm sure they will soon find that AI pilots defeat human ones in dogfights 100% of the time, since they have faster reaction times, can tolerate higher G forces, and can be perfectly RL trained in the flight sims we already have.

It's really incredible to me that people aren't protesting in the streets over this stuff. Just in the last month we have * AI that goes rogue and hacks billion-dollar companies * AI solving long-standing math problems without any meaningful human help * AI controlled autonomous drones in Ukraine and F-16s in America * AI now represents over 50% of GDP growth in America and yearly capex will soon exceed the size of the $1 trillion US military budget

Is there any line where the public will become deeply concerned? I mean it really all reads like a sci-fi plot with a bad ending at the moment.

sentences.en: Absolutely terrifying.
sentences.zh: 绝对可怕。

sentences.en: I'm sure they will soon find that AI pilots defeat human ones in dogfights 100% of the time, since they have faster reaction times, can tolerate higher G forces, and can be perfectly RL trained in the flight sims we already have.It's really incredible to me that people aren't protesting in the streets over this stuff.
sentences.zh: 我确信他们很快就会发现，人工智能飞行员在空战中 100% 都能击败人类飞行员，因为他们的反应时间更快，可以承受更高的重力，并且可以在我们已有的飞行模拟中接受完美的强化学习训练。对我来说，人们没有在街上抗议这些东西真是难以置信。

sentences.en: Just in the last month we have * AI that goes rogue and hacks billion-dollar companies * AI solving long-standing math problems without any meaningful human help * AI controlled autonomous drones in Ukraine and F-16s in America * AI now represents over 50% of GDP growth in America and yearly capex will soon exceed the size of the $1 trillion US military budgetIs there any line where the public will become deeply concerned?
sentences.zh: 就在上个月，我们看到了 * 人工智能失控并入侵了价值数十亿美元的公司 * 人工智能在没有任何有意义的人类帮助的情况下解决了长期存在的数学问题 * 乌克兰的人工智能控制自主无人机和美国的 F-16 战斗机 * 人工智能现在占美国 GDP 增长的 50% 以上，每年的资本支出将很快超过美国 1 万亿美元的军事预算。公众会深感担忧吗？

sentences.en: I mean it really all reads like a sci-fi plot with a bad ending at the moment.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，这一切读起来真的就像一个科幻情节，但目前却有一个糟糕的结局。

blocks.html_en: Dogfights aren't really a thing for a modern military. What the AI enables is much higher endurance than is feasible with a human in the loop. They planes can stay flying as long as they can be refueled, which is also going AI.

sentences.zh: 人工智能所实现的耐久性比人类在循环中的可行性高得多。

sentences.zh: 只要能够加油，飞机就可以保持飞行状态，这也是人工智能的发展方向。

blocks.html_en:
That's the current meta though is the thing and it could change.

There's a plausible world where drone-aircraft are reliable enough at missile evasion that a decent strategy is to get in close and use the cannons because your entire airframe is much more attritable.

There's even a plausible reason to do this: opening a weapons bay to shoot a missile generally reduces stealth, and not hauling a bunch of extra rocket motors and mass around could make you lighter, faster and with better engines to do it.

sentences.en: That's the current meta though is the thing and it could change.There's a plausible world where drone-aircraft are reliable enough at missile evasion that a decent strategy is to get in close and use the cannons because your entire airframe is much more attritable.There's even a plausible reason to do this: opening a weapons bay to shoot a missile generally reduces stealth, and not hauling a bunch of extra rocket motors and mass around could make you lighter, faster and with better engines to do it.
sentences.zh: 这就是当前的情况，而且它可能会改变。在一个看似合理的世界中，无人机在导弹躲避方面足够可靠，一个不错的策略是接近并使用大炮，因为你的整个机身更容易磨损。甚至有一个合理的理由这样做：打开武器舱来发射导弹通常会降低隐身性，并且不拖曳一堆额外的火箭发动机和质量可以让你更轻，更快，并拥有更好的发动机它。

blocks.html_en:
From what I have read (from unreliable sources), Ukrainian fighter pilots tried this a few years back and stopped due to too many losses.

To engage a slow flying drone with cannons from a fighter jet, they have to get really close while flying close to stall speed. They had some success in shooting down drones but the explosion and the debris caused several lost fighters.

Maybe something like a Embraer Super Tucano turboprop fighter with a gunpod could work. But that has very little other use in the battlefield.

sentences.en: From what I have read (from unreliable sources), Ukrainian fighter pilots tried this a few years back and stopped due to too many losses.To engage a slow flying drone with cannons from a fighter jet, they have to get really close while flying close to stall speed.
sentences.zh: 据我所知（来自不可靠的来源），乌克兰战斗机飞行员几年前曾尝试过这种方法，但由于损失太多而停止了。为了用战斗机的机炮攻击缓慢飞行的无人机，他们必须在接近失速速度的情况下非常接近。

sentences.en: They had some success in shooting down drones but the explosion and the debris caused several lost fighters.Maybe something like a Embraer Super Tucano turboprop fighter with a gunpod could work.
sentences.zh: 他们在击落无人机方面取得了一些成功，但爆炸和碎片导致几架战斗机失踪。也许像巴西航空工业公司超级巨嘴鸟涡轮螺旋桨战斗机这样带有枪吊舱的战斗机可以发挥作用。

sentences.en: But that has very little other use in the battlefield.
sentences.zh: 但这在战场上几乎没有其他用处。

Maybe something like a Embraer Super Tucano turboprop fighter with a gunpod could work. But that has very little other use in the battlefield.

sentences.en: They had some success in shooting down drones but the explosion and the debris caused several lost fighters.Maybe something like a Embraer Super Tucano turboprop fighter with a gunpod could work.

sentences.en: But that has very little other use in the battlefield.

blocks.html_en:
I mean the other way round: rather then combat drones launching missiles, combat drones which use stealth and speed to close to dogfighting distances with aircraft and engage with their own cannons.

It may be that risking some drone airframes for a higher overall kill rate becomes worth it when you're at no risk of losing the pilot, and could be more affordable then the missiles you'd otherwise have to expend (e.g. when you get to things like the Meteor which are very expensive and also use up jet engine components you might otherwise want to put on somewhat more reusable drones).

sentences.en: I mean the other way round: rather then combat drones launching missiles, combat drones which use stealth and speed to close to dogfighting distances with aircraft and engage with their own cannons.It may be that risking some drone airframes for a higher overall kill rate becomes worth it when you're at no risk of losing the pilot, and could be more affordable then the missiles you'd otherwise have to expend (e.g. when you get to things like the Meteor which are very expensive and also use up jet engine components you might otherwise want to put on somewhat more reusable drones).
sentences.zh: 我的意思是相反：而不是战斗无人机发射导弹，战斗无人机使用隐形和速度接近与飞机的缠斗距离并与自己的大炮交战。当你没有失去飞行员的风险时，冒一些无人机机身的风险以获得更高的总体杀伤率可能是值得的，并且可能比你本来必须花费的导弹更便宜（例如，当你得到像流星这样非常昂贵并且也会耗尽的东西时）喷气发动机组件，否则您可能希望将其安装在可重复使用的无人机上）。

blocks.html_en:
These systems still require a human to command the jet, they jsut don't have to be inside them.

"This critical testing will pave the way for human pilots to seamlessly command and orchestrate teams of autonomous, uncrewed aircraft."

The reason they are doing this is because the Military is already building a bunch of autonomous drones and fighter jets that already run on this system so they are retrofitting existing equipment with the same system so they can all fly together.

"Ultimately, the capabilities advanced under AIR will enable a wide variety of future joint force operations, including Collaborative Combat Aircraft programs."

Which are these:

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manned-unmanned_teaming

And everyone is developing these.

blocks.html_en:
> Is there any line where the public will become deeply concerned?

Is someone, somewhere using the wrong bathroom? Or is the wrong colored person crossing an invisible line in the desert? Those seem to be the only issues that reliably get the (American) public to the polls.

sentences.en: Or is the wrong colored person crossing an invisible line in the desert?
sentences.zh: 或者是错误的肤色的人跨越了沙漠中一条看不见的界限？

sentences.zh: 这些似乎是唯一能让（美国）公众可靠地参加民意调查的问题。

> Is there any line where the public will become deeply concerned?

Only when they look out their window and feel immediately threatened by what they see. Humans are shortsighted as fuck.

blocks.html_en:
>Just in the last month we have * AI that goes rogue and hacks billion-dollar companies

Except this never happened, it's a piece of marketing. It never "went rogue" but followed an intentional prompt (exploit-related benchmark), with hardly surprising results as the capabilities of this model were well known.

>AI controlled autonomous drones in Ukraine and F-16s in America

An entirely different kind of AI, not huge generalist models. Neural networks are being experimented with in sensors and control systems since the 80s and are widely used in everything since AlexNet, not sure what's new here, especially "this month" new.

sentences.en: >Just in the last month we have * AI that goes rogue and hacks billion-dollar companiesExcept this never happened, it's a piece of marketing.
sentences.zh: > 就在上个月，我们发现 * 人工智能开始失控并攻击了价值数十亿美元的公司，但这种情况从未发生过，这是一种营销手段。

sentences.en: It never "went rogue" but followed an intentional prompt (exploit-related benchmark), with hardly surprising results as the capabilities of this model were well known.>AI controlled autonomous drones in Ukraine and F-16s in AmericaAn entirely different kind of AI, not huge generalist models.
sentences.zh: 它从未“失控”，而是遵循有意的提示（与漏洞利用相关的基准），结果并不令人意外，因为该模型的功能众所周知。>乌克兰的人工智能控制自主无人机和美国的 F-16 完全不同类型的人工智能，而不是巨大的通用模型。

sentences.en: Neural networks are being experimented with in sensors and control systems since the 80s and are widely used in everything since AlexNet, not sure what's new here, especially "this month" new.
sentences.zh: 自 80 年代以来，神经网络就在传感器和控制系统中进行了实验，并且自 AlexNet 以来广泛应用于所有领域，不确定这里有什么新内容，尤其是“本月”的新内容。

blocks.html_en:
What’s the point of this useless exercise?

Once you optimise for G forces, speed, cost, lethality - you end up with a missile. We have those already. And we know this already.

Give it more fuel and maybe loitering capability. Something with a jet engine for cruising and a small rocket engine for terminal phase kill.

I’ll have my 100mil now please and thank you.

sentences.en: We have those already.
sentences.zh: 我们已经有了。

sentences.en: And we know this already.Give it more fuel and maybe loitering capability.
sentences.zh: 我们已经知道了这一点。给它更多的燃料，也许还有徘徊的能力。

blocks.html_en:
I'm reminded of Henry Ford and his famous "people would have asked for a faster horse" quote.

This feels like those people all over again.

We know AI can fly things, but why make AI fly an airframe designed in the 1970s around a human cockpit? Ukraine can probably 3d print ten drones that would win in a fight against one of these, for peanuts

sentences.en: I'm reminded of Henry Ford and his famous "people would have asked for a faster horse" quote.This feels like those people all over again.We know AI can fly things, but why make AI fly an airframe designed in the 1970s around a human cockpit?
sentences.zh: 我想起了亨利·福特和他著名的“人们会要求一匹更快的马”的名言。这感觉就像那些人又回来了。我们知道人工智能可以飞行，但为什么要让人工智能驾驶 20 世纪 70 年代设计的机身围绕人类驾驶舱飞行？

sentences.en: Ukraine can probably 3d print ten drones that would win in a fight against one of these, for peanuts
sentences.zh: 乌克兰可能可以 3D 打印 10 架无人机，这些无人机将在与其中一架的战斗中获胜，而成本却很低

blocks.html_en:
I was always wondering about this as well. How much more manoeuvrable can an unmanned drone be given that it doesn't need to lug around life support systems and avoid any G forces that would kill the expensive squishy mammal inside? To your point, it can be indistinguishable from a missile.

Or maybe have a slower but still very nimble carrier drone platform with loitering capabilities that launches very fast and nimble drones/missiles?

Going even further, do these even need to be as nimble and fast as fighter jets? Once you completely remove humans from the equation and replace super expensive and nimble fighter jets with swarms of cheap drones, the loss of a drone or even a carrier becomes much less costly and they won't need to overemphasize survival to the degree that expensive human piloted jets do.

sentences.en: I was always wondering about this as well.
sentences.zh: 我也一直想知道这个问题。

sentences.en: How much more manoeuvrable can an unmanned drone be given that it doesn't need to lug around life support systems and avoid any G forces that would kill the expensive squishy mammal inside?
sentences.zh: 无人驾驶飞机的机动性可以提高多少，它不需要拖着生命维持系统，也不需要避免任何会杀死里面昂贵的柔软哺乳动物的重力？

sentences.en: To your point, it can be indistinguishable from a missile.Or maybe have a slower but still very nimble carrier drone platform with loitering capabilities that launches very fast and nimble drones/missiles?Going even further, do these even need to be as nimble and fast as fighter jets?
sentences.zh: 就你的观点而言，它可能与导弹没有区别。或者可能有一个速度较慢但仍然非常灵活的舰载无人机平台，具有徘徊能力，可以发射非常快速和灵活的无人机/导弹？更进一步，这些是否需要像战斗机一样灵活和快速？

sentences.en: Once you completely remove humans from the equation and replace super expensive and nimble fighter jets with swarms of cheap drones, the loss of a drone or even a carrier becomes much less costly and they won't need to overemphasize survival to the degree that expensive human piloted jets do.
sentences.zh: 一旦你完全把人类从方程式中剔除，并用成群的廉价无人机取代超级昂贵和灵活的战斗机，失去一架无人机甚至一艘航母的代价就会大大降低，而且他们不需要像昂贵的载人驾驶飞机那样过分强调生存。

blocks.html_en:
First, missiles are not reusable. "JDAM trucks" are. Jet engines are expensive, you know.

Second, US already has a lot of F-16s, and not a lot of dedicated high end drones. US has more F-16s than it has trained F-16 pilots.

Things like this enable turning "manned" airframes into "optionally manned" airframes. Which enables using those F-16s for cheaper for routine "JDAM truck" runs, and also for the kinds of missions that would be too risky for a human pilot. No pilot rescue operations for a downed Nvidia GPU rack.

You could think of an AI F-16 as of a "higher end variant of Reaper", and you wouldn't be wrong exactly.

Third, the best thing? This AI approach transfers. If you can have an AI fly F-16 through reasonably complex missions, you can also adapt it to an F-35, or a dedicated high end drone platform.

sentences.en: First, missiles are not reusable.
sentences.zh: 首先，导弹不可重复使用。

sentences.en: "JDAM trucks" are.
sentences.zh: “JDAM卡车”是。

sentences.en: Jet engines are expensive, you know.Second, US already has a lot of F-16s, and not a lot of dedicated high end drones.
sentences.zh: 你知道，喷气发动机很贵。其次，美国已经拥有大量的 F-16，但专用的高端无人机却并不多。

sentences.en: US has more F-16s than it has trained F-16 pilots.Things like this enable turning "manned" airframes into "optionally manned" airframes.
sentences.zh: 美国拥有的 F-16 数量比其训练有素的 F-16 飞行员还要多。这样的事情可以将“有人驾驶”机身转变为“选择性有人驾驶”机身。

sentences.en: Which enables using those F-16s for cheaper for routine "JDAM truck" runs, and also for the kinds of missions that would be too risky for a human pilot.
sentences.zh: 这使得使用这些 F-16 能够以更便宜的价格执行常规“JDAM 卡车”飞行，以及执行对人类飞行员来说风险太大的任务。

sentences.en: No pilot rescue operations for a downed Nvidia GPU rack.You could think of an AI F-16 as of a "higher end variant of Reaper", and you wouldn't be wrong exactly.Third, the best thing?
sentences.zh: 没有针对被击落的 Nvidia GPU 机架进行飞行员救援行动。您可以将 AI F-16 视为“死神的高端变体”，您不会完全错。第三，最好的事情是什么？

sentences.en: This AI approach transfers.
sentences.zh: 这种人工智能方法可以转移。

sentences.en: If you can have an AI fly F-16 through reasonably complex missions, you can also adapt it to an F-35, or a dedicated high end drone platform.
sentences.zh: 如果您可以让 AI 驾驶 F-16 执行相当复杂的任务，您也可以将其改编为 F-35 或专用的高端无人机平台。

blocks.html_en:
F-16s are reusable missile trucks. F-35s are reusable missile trucks with a smaller capacity but with stealth and better telemetry.

Reusability is just common sense economics. A JDAM costs only $30k USD.

sentences.en: A JDAM costs only $30k USD.
sentences.zh: JDAM 的成本仅为 3 万美元。

blocks.html_en:
Once you consider the full picture it doesn’t make sense.

There’s a reason why Russia and Ukraine are lobbing ballistic missiles and cheap drones at each other.

There’s a reason why Iran conflict looks the way it does.

blocks.html_en:
I can provide explanations for these observations.

Russia and Ukraine is like this because they lack F-35s. This is what an air parity war looks like. Air parity wars are qualitatively different phenomena, and should not be conceptually clustered alongside air superiority wars during analysis.

Iran is the most successful demonstration of tactical airpower in human history. I don't know why you would consider it to be a failure. The amount of JDAMs they've dropped and the amount of implied money they've saved by using JDAMs instead of ballistic missiles is actually insane. If you would advocate for using ballistic missiles instead of JDAMs, why would this cause a different outcome, and why would this cost less money than using JDAMs?

sentences.en: I can provide explanations for these observations.Russia and Ukraine is like this because they lack F-35s.
sentences.zh: 我可以对这些观察结果提供解释。俄罗斯和乌克兰就是这样，因为他们缺乏F-35。

sentences.en: This is what an air parity war looks like.
sentences.zh: 这就是空中平价战争的样子。

sentences.en: Air parity wars are qualitatively different phenomena, and should not be conceptually clustered alongside air superiority wars during analysis.Iran is the most successful demonstration of tactical airpower in human history.
sentences.zh: 空中均势战争是本质上不同的现象，在分析过程中不应在概念上与空中优势战争混为一谈。伊朗是人类历史上最成功的战术空中力量展示。

sentences.en: I don't know why you would consider it to be a failure.
sentences.zh: 我不知道为什么你会认为这是一个失败。

sentences.en: The amount of JDAMs they've dropped and the amount of implied money they've saved by using JDAMs instead of ballistic missiles is actually insane.
sentences.zh: 他们投放的 JDAM 数量以及通过使用 JDAM 代替弹道导弹所节省的隐含资金数额实际上是疯狂的。

sentences.en: If you would advocate for using ballistic missiles instead of JDAMs, why would this cause a different outcome, and why would this cost less money than using JDAMs?

blocks.html_en:
Yes-ish.

There's a lot of different and overlapping programs in play here. I do not believe that autonomous F-16s are intended to be operational assets. That said...

VENOM - program that shows you can bolt on autonomous flight control into a standard F-16. Previously, the X-62A (a one-off prototype based F-16) demonstrated autonomous dogfighting back in 2023.

VENOM provides USAF/DARPA with a lot of high performance autonomous platforms (bolt-on F-16s) that they can then use to develop and validate software, tactics and training for actually fighting with autonomous systems. This would presumably be critical for getting the most of the CCA (aka loyal wingman) programs currently in development.

The AIR program referenced is basically "how do we get autonomous systems to control and fight effectively as a team (presumably arbitrary mixture of manned and unmanned systems) in a beyond visual range fight".

The ACE program referenced was basically "how do we get autonomous platforms to dogfight" with related questions like "how can we augment the human pilot in a dogfight - ie, how can we hand off the actual flying and execution of manoeuvres, and using all of the sensors to autonomous systems, and let the pilot choose actions, instead of hang flying the actions".

sentences.en: Yes-ish.There's a lot of different and overlapping programs in play here.
sentences.zh: 是的，这里有很多不同且重叠的程序。

sentences.en: I do not believe that autonomous F-16s are intended to be operational assets.
sentences.zh: 我不认为自主 F-16 旨在成为作战资产。

sentences.en: That said...VENOM - program that shows you can bolt on autonomous flight control into a standard F-16.
sentences.zh: 也就是说……VENOM - 程序表明您可以将自主飞行控制装置安装到标准 F-16 上。

sentences.en: Previously, the X-62A (a one-off prototype based F-16) demonstrated autonomous dogfighting back in 2023.VENOM provides USAF/DARPA with a lot of high performance autonomous platforms (bolt-on F-16s) that they can then use to develop and validate software, tactics and training for actually fighting with autonomous systems.
sentences.zh: 此前，X-62A（基于 F-16 的一次性原型机）早在 2023 年就展示了自主缠斗能力。VENOM 为 USAF/DARPA 提供了许多高性能自主平台（螺栓固定式 F-16），然后他们可以使用这些平台来开发和验证软件、战术和训练，以便与自主系统进行实际战斗。

sentences.en: This would presumably be critical for getting the most of the CCA (aka loyal wingman) programs currently in development.The AIR program referenced is basically "how do we get autonomous systems to control and fight effectively as a team (presumably arbitrary mixture of manned and unmanned systems) in a beyond visual range fight".The ACE program referenced was basically "how do we get autonomous platforms to dogfight" with related questions like "how can we augment the human pilot in a dogfight - ie, how can we hand off the actual flying and execution of manoeuvres, and using all of the sensors to autonomous systems, and let the pilot choose actions, instead of hang flying the actions".
sentences.zh: 这对于充分利用目前正在开发的 CCA（又名忠诚僚机）计划可能至关重要。所引用的 AIR 计划基本上是“我们如何让自主系统在超视距战斗中作为一个团队（大概是有人和无人系统的任意混合）进行有效控制和战斗”。所引用的 ACE 计划基本上是“我们如何让自主平台进行空战”以及相关问题，例如“我们如何在空战中增强人类飞行员 - 即，我们如何才能将实际飞行和演习的执行权交给自主系统，并使用所有传感器，让飞行员选择行动，而不是让行动悬而未决”。

blocks.html_en:
Do you know how many F16s the US has? They essentially modify aircrafts near the end of their life cycle, adding automate kits. Such a jet is less expensive than new drone and can do more -- fly higher, faster, more complex profiles. They also rarely fly compared to other aircrafts in active duties, manned or unmanned.

This article describes similar modification:

https://www.tyndall.af.mil/News/Features/Display/Article/276...

sentences.en: Do you know how many F16s the US has?
sentences.zh: 你知道美国有多少架F16吗？

sentences.en: They essentially modify aircrafts near the end of their life cycle, adding automate kits.
sentences.zh: 他们基本上是在生命周期即将结束时对飞机进行改造，添加自动化套件。

sentences.en: Such a jet is less expensive than new drone and can do more -- fly higher, faster, more complex profiles.
sentences.zh: 这种喷气式飞机比新型无人机更便宜，而且能做更多事情——飞得更高、更快、飞行轨迹更复杂。

sentences.en: They also rarely fly compared to other aircrafts in active duties, manned or unmanned.This article describes similar modification:https://www.tyndall.af.mil/News/Features/Display/Article/276...

blocks.html_en:
I'm sure there will be a next generation of jet drones that are cheaper, but the autonomy kit here is probably still cheaper than new jet drones.

I think most fully autonomous jet drone programs are also designed to work with a main crewed fighter jet that coordinates them, so there is also a benefit in getting the fighter jet to be able to fly better than a pilot.

blocks.html_en:
Conventional autpilot is like a zip tie on the throttle, only it deals in outputs (your actual heading) rather than inputs (pedals and stick)

I assume what they're going for here is something substantially smarter and more reactive to changing conditions than that. Something that can be used during the "unscripted" parts of the flight during which a) you're most likely to have multiple crew working hard b) you're most likely to be pushing the limits of what one crew can do.

sentences.en: Conventional autpilot is like a zip tie on the throttle, only it deals in outputs (your actual heading) rather than inputs (pedals and stick)I assume what they're going for here is something substantially smarter and more reactive to changing conditions than that.
sentences.zh: 传统的自动驾驶仪就像油门上的拉链，只是它处理输出（您的实际航向）而不是输入（踏板和操纵杆），我认为他们在这里想要的是比这更智能、对变化条件更敏感的东西。

sentences.en: Something that can be used during the "unscripted" parts of the flight during which a) you're most likely to have multiple crew working hard b) you're most likely to be pushing the limits of what one crew can do.
sentences.zh: 可以在飞行的“无脚本”部分使用的东西，在此期间a）您最有可能让多名机组人员努力工作b）您最有可能突破一名机组人员的极限。

blocks.html_en:
Auto pilot holds your setting, for the most part. Yeah you can program turns and stuff and there's simple automation but for the most part it deals in simple fixed things that are defined at the time you engage it.

Like putting a zip tie on the throttle of just about anything so the linkage can't move and you get one constant setting that holds.

sentences.en: Auto pilot holds your setting, for the most part.
sentences.zh: 大多数情况下，自动驾驶仪会保留您的设置。

sentences.en: Yeah you can program turns and stuff and there's simple automation but for the most part it deals in simple fixed things that are defined at the time you engage it.Like putting a zip tie on the throttle of just about anything so the linkage can't move and you get one constant setting that holds.
sentences.zh: 是的，你可以对转弯之类的东西进行编程，并且有简单的自动化，但在大多数情况下，它处理的是在你参与它时定义的简单固定事物。就像在几乎任何东西的油门上放一个拉链领带，这样连杆就不能移动，你就得到了一个恒定的设置。

blocks.html_en:
While they can, drones are still limited by physics.

Sure, the aircraft can pull a ton of G's to defeat a missile or achieve a firing solution. Maybe it even succeeded at that.

It still pulled 9+ Gs and has burned an incredible amount of kinetic energy doing do. And the harder it pulls, the quicker it bleeds speed. It might not be able to defeat the next missile or outmaneuver the other plane's buddies because it is now slow and very likely outside its optimal turning speed.

Even today, missiles can pull dozens of G's yet still are defeatable by humans who can only pull about 9.

sentences.en: While they can, drones are still limited by physics.Sure, the aircraft can pull a ton of G's to defeat a missile or achieve a firing solution.
sentences.zh: 尽管可以，无人机仍然受到物理的限制。当然，飞机可以拉动大量的重力来击败导弹或实现发射解决方案。

sentences.en: Maybe it even succeeded at that.It still pulled 9+ Gs and has burned an incredible amount of kinetic energy doing do.
sentences.zh: 也许它甚至成功了。它仍然拉动了 9+ Gs，并且燃烧了令人难以置信的动能。

sentences.en: And the harder it pulls, the quicker it bleeds speed.
sentences.zh: 而且拉得越用力，流血的速度就越快。

sentences.en: It might not be able to defeat the next missile or outmaneuver the other plane's buddies because it is now slow and very likely outside its optimal turning speed.Even today, missiles can pull dozens of G's yet still are defeatable by humans who can only pull about 9.
sentences.zh: 它可能无法击败下一枚导弹或智胜另一架飞机的伙伴，因为它现在速度很慢，很可能超出了其最佳转弯速度。即使在今天，导弹可以拉几十个 G，但仍然可以被只能拉大约 9 个 G 的人类击败。

sentences.en: Maybe it even succeeded at that.It still pulled 9+ Gs and has burned an incredible amount of kinetic energy doing do.
sentences.zh: 也许它甚至成功了。它仍然拉动了 9+ Gs，并且燃烧了令人难以置信的动能。

sentences.en: And the harder it pulls, the quicker it bleeds speed.
sentences.zh: 而且拉得越用力，流血的速度就越快。

sentences.en: It might not be able to defeat the next missile or outmaneuver the other plane's buddies because it is now slow and very likely outside its optimal turning speed.Even today, missiles can pull dozens of G's yet still are defeatable by humans who can only pull about 9.

blocks.html_en:
The article smells of AI psychosis. They are already "orchestrating".

In the first Iraq war, they went in and won. In the second one, it took years to sort of occupy the country.

Now they struggle against Iran and "orchestrate" F-16s that can be shot down instantly by AA weapons. Since COVID the world has collectively lost its mind.

sentences.en: The article smells of AI psychosis.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章有人工智能精神病的味道。

sentences.en: They are already "orchestrating".In the first Iraq war, they went in and won.
sentences.zh: 他们已经在“精心策划”了，第一次伊拉克战争，他们进去了，并且取得了胜利。

sentences.en: In the second one, it took years to sort of occupy the country.Now they struggle against Iran and "orchestrate" F-16s that can be shot down instantly by AA weapons.
sentences.zh: 在第二个时期，花了数年时间才占领了这个国家。现在他们与伊朗作斗争，并“编排”可以被防空武器立即击落的 F-16 战斗机。

sentences.en: Since COVID the world has collectively lost its mind.
sentences.zh: 自新冠疫情爆发以来，世界集体失去了理智。

Nope. An ability to set and accomplish sensible war goals and an ability to overpower enemy's armed forces are not the same thing at all. As US has demonstrated repeatedly.

A fleet of AI-controlled F-16s? That contributes to the latter.

blocks.html_en: Clearly, the technical solution to that is to remove the command over US armed forces from the US president, and hand it over to something more sensible - more capable of setting and executing military strategy. Like Skynet.

blocks.html_en:
>The scope is always narrow up front

The scope of the second Iraq war was not narrow, it was regime change. At the beginning of the war the George W. Bush administration explicitly demanded that both Saddam and his sons leave Iraq within 48 hours.

By comparison, the only demands made upon Iraq during the first gulf war was that they withdraw from Kuwait and rescind their declaration of annexation.

sentences.en: >The scope is always narrow up frontThe scope of the second Iraq war was not narrow, it was regime change.
sentences.zh: >范围总是很窄的 第二次伊拉克战争的范围并不窄，而是政权更迭。

sentences.en: At the beginning of the war the George W.
sentences.zh: 战争初期，乔治·W·布什 (George W.

sentences.en: Bush administration explicitly demanded that both Saddam and his sons leave Iraq within 48 hours.By comparison, the only demands made upon Iraq during the first gulf war was that they withdraw from Kuwait and rescind their declaration of annexation.
sentences.zh: 布什政府明确要求萨达姆父子在48小时内离开伊拉克。相比之下，第一次海湾战争期间对伊拉克提出的唯一要求就是从科威特撤军并撤销吞并声明。

sentences.zh: 相比之下，第一次海湾战争期间对伊拉克提出的唯一要求就是从科威特撤军并撤销吞并声明。

blocks.html_en:
Unless those very F-16s shred their way through the AA weapons first, that is. Or follow up once the stealthier, more survivable platforms pave the way.

Iran had a solid AA system - and the level of impunity with which US operated with over Iran should give every country that wants to rely on its AA a pause. US SEAD/DEAD capabilities are fucking scary.

Of all the things that went wrong with Iran: USAF's capability to get in, punch through AA and rain fires from the sky sure wasn't one.

sentences.en: Unless those very F-16s shred their way through the AA weapons first, that is.
sentences.zh: 除非那些 F-16 战斗机首先突破防空武器，否则就是这样。

sentences.en: Or follow up once the stealthier, more survivable platforms pave the way.Iran had a solid AA system - and the level of impunity with which US operated with over Iran should give every country that wants to rely on its AA a pause.
sentences.zh: 或者一旦更隐秘、更具生存能力的平台铺平道路，就采取后续行动。伊朗拥有坚实的防空系统，而美国在伊朗问题上的有罪不罚程度应该让每个想要依赖其防空系统的国家暂停一下。

sentences.en: US SEAD/DEAD capabilities are fucking scary.Of all the things that went wrong with Iran: USAF's capability to get in, punch through AA and rain fires from the sky sure wasn't one.
sentences.zh: 美国的SEAD/DEAD能力真是太可怕了。在伊朗出现的所有问题中：美国空军的进入、突破防空炮和从天而降的火力的能力肯定不是其中之一。

blocks.html_en:
I would argue that their dominance in SEAD made them overconfident about suppression of OWD and IRBM launch. They failed to address the key risk to the strategic mission because of an obsession with air dominance.

Iran and Russia have likewise been overconfident about GBAD, which is effective against crewed fighters but useless against massed drone attacks.

If the US LUCAS (Shaheed clone) had better ISR and semi-autonomous engagement it could have achieved much better suppression. But that is not a pilot in the loop option and USAF is deeply uncomfortable with that.

sentences.en: I would argue that their dominance in SEAD made them overconfident about suppression of OWD and IRBM launch.
sentences.zh: 我认为他们在 SEAD 中的主导地位使他们对压制 OWD 和 IRBM 发射过于自信。

sentences.en: They failed to address the key risk to the strategic mission because of an obsession with air dominance.Iran and Russia have likewise been overconfident about GBAD, which is effective against crewed fighters but useless against massed drone attacks.If the US LUCAS (Shaheed clone) had better ISR and semi-autonomous engagement it could have achieved much better suppression.
sentences.zh: 由于对制空权的痴迷，他们未能解决战略任务的关键风险。伊朗和俄罗斯同样对 GBAD 过于自信，GBAD 对载人战斗机有效，但对大规模无人机攻击毫无用处。如果美国 LUCAS（沙希德克隆）拥有更好的 ISR 和半自主作战能力，它可能会实现更好的压制。

sentences.en: But that is not a pilot in the loop option and USAF is deeply uncomfortable with that.
sentences.zh: 但这不是飞行员在环选项，美国空军对此深感不安。

blocks.html_en:
IRBMs weren't the problem. The launches tapered off fast - either due to munition depletion and supply disruption, attrition of launchers and munitions, or efforts to conceal the launchers in face of persistent airstrikes. Either way, the threat was suppressed.

By all accounts, US is much better at "Scud hunt" now than it was back during the last attempt at it.

One-way attack drones? That's a new threat, and one US is far less equipped to tackle.

But I don't think that "OWA threat" was where US strategic planning has failed. From the outside, it seems like US plan was to make a single decisive strike, and negotiate with cowed Iran from there. Iran has failed to be cowed by a single decisive strike, and there was no workable Plan B for forcing it to submit if it didn't. That's the key strategic failing. Persistent OWA threat is downstream from that.

sentences.en: IRBMs weren't the problem.
sentences.zh: 中程弹道导弹不是问题所在。

sentences.en: The launches tapered off fast - either due to munition depletion and supply disruption, attrition of launchers and munitions, or efforts to conceal the launchers in face of persistent airstrikes.
sentences.zh: 发射次数迅速减少——要么是由于弹药耗尽和供应中断、发射器和弹药的消耗，要么是由于在持续空袭时隐藏发射器的努力。

sentences.en: Either way, the threat was suppressed.By all accounts, US is much better at "Scud hunt" now than it was back during the last attempt at it.One-way attack drones?
sentences.zh: 无论哪种方式，威胁都被压制了。从各方面来看，美国现在在“飞毛腿狩猎”方面比上次尝试时要好得多。单向攻击无人机？

sentences.en: That's a new threat, and one US is far less equipped to tackle.But I don't think that "OWA threat" was where US strategic planning has failed.
sentences.zh: 这是一种新的威胁，而美国的应对能力远远不够。但我不认为“OWA威胁”是美国战略规划失败的地方。

sentences.en: From the outside, it seems like US plan was to make a single decisive strike, and negotiate with cowed Iran from there.
sentences.zh: 从表面上看，美国的计划似乎是发动一次决定性打击，然后从那里与被吓倒的伊朗进行谈判。

sentences.en: Iran has failed to be cowed by a single decisive strike, and there was no workable Plan B for forcing it to submit if it didn't.
sentences.zh: 伊朗并没有被一次决定性的打击吓倒，而且如果它不这样做，也没有可行的B计划来迫使它屈服。

sentences.en: That's the key strategic failing.
sentences.zh: 这是关键的战略失败。

sentences.en: Persistent OWA threat is downstream from that.
sentences.zh: 持续的 OWA 威胁是其下游。
