blocks.html_en:
The IRBMs are a huge problem. IRGC is using them sparingly, but they were not destroyed.

USAF has actually failed when it comes to 'scud hunt'. It doesn't have the combat ISR or strike capability it needs. As a result, the US basing and resupply situation is chaotic. The loss of the THAAD battery is extremely problematic, which is why they had to move one from SK.

The OWD capability allows Iran to attack tankers anywhere in the Gulf. Even with a surge in GCC state IAMD capability, ships cannot be protected. The failure to address this capability in planning was critical.

Obviously the DoD leadership is cowed by Hegseth and wouldn't give a true assessment, so we have a lions led by donkeys situation, but the operational plan was still bad.

sentences.en: The IRBMs are a huge problem.
sentences.zh: 中程弹道导弹是一个大问题。

sentences.en: IRGC is using them sparingly, but they were not destroyed.USAF has actually failed when it comes to 'scud hunt'.
sentences.zh: 伊朗革命卫队很少使用它们，但它们并没有被摧毁。美国空军在“飞毛腿狩猎”方面实际上失败了。

sentences.en: It doesn't have the combat ISR or strike capability it needs.
sentences.zh: 它不具备所需的战斗情监侦或打击能力。

sentences.en: As a result, the US basing and resupply situation is chaotic.
sentences.zh: 导致美军基地和补给局势混乱。

sentences.en: The loss of the THAAD battery is extremely problematic, which is why they had to move one from SK.The OWD capability allows Iran to attack tankers anywhere in the Gulf.
sentences.zh: THAAD 电池的损失是一个极其严重的问题，这就是为什么他们必须从 SK 转移一个。 OWD 能力使伊朗能够攻击海湾任何地方的油轮。

sentences.en: Even with a surge in GCC state IAMD capability, ships cannot be protected.
sentences.zh: 即使海湾合作委员会国家的 IAMD 能力激增，船只也无法得到保护。

sentences.en: The failure to address this capability in planning was critical.Obviously the DoD leadership is cowed by Hegseth and wouldn't give a true assessment, so we have a lions led by donkeys situation, but the operational plan was still bad.
sentences.zh: 在规划中未能解决这种能力问题是至关重要的。显然，国防部领导层被赫格斯吓倒了，不会给出真正的评估，所以我们遇到了驴子领导的狮子的情况，但作战计划仍然很糟糕。

blocks.html_en: The OWD capability allows Iran to attack tankers anywhere in the Gulf. Even with a surge in GCC state IAMD capability, ships cannot be protected. The failure to address this capability in planning was critical.

sentences.en: The OWD capability allows Iran to attack tankers anywhere in the Gulf.
sentences.zh: OWD 能力使伊朗能够攻击海湾任何地方的油轮。

sentences.en: Even with a surge in GCC state IAMD capability, ships cannot be protected.
sentences.zh: 即使海湾合作委员会国家的 IAMD 能力激增，船只也无法得到保护。

sentences.en: The failure to address this capability in planning was critical.
sentences.zh: 未能在规划中解决这种能力问题至关重要。

blocks.html_en: Obviously the DoD leadership is cowed by Hegseth and wouldn't give a true assessment, so we have a lions led by donkeys situation, but the operational plan was still bad.

blocks.html_en:
The joke is Russia has always been incompetent.

McCain was actually on the money in 2008. He was in the audience when Putin gave that famous "warning to the west" speech in Germany [1].

The quip that Russia is a "gas station with nuclear weapons" about sums it up. The United States being weak or not really has nothing to do with it. They counted on Zelensky cutting and running, the attempted decapitation of Kyiv failed. Russia is swirling the toilet. Without the energy reserves their oligarchs would be worthless. Since most of what is actually valuable about their country is land (where the resources sit) that is sparsely inhabited the end state of China controlling most of what is now Russia is inevitable. By failing in Europe and tying himself to China, Putin has ensured Russian power will continue to erode. Of course, it's not great for China, having to deal with two nuclear armed pariahs on their borders.

The United States is truly blessed simply by being two oceans away from anyone who could remotely challenge it. Properly understood, the Monroe (or "Donroe" doctrine) is less about foreign intervention and more an understanding that if a peer power ever emerged in the Western Hemisphere, most of what appears to make the United States singular would rapidly disappear.

[1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hQ58Yv6kP44

sentences.en: The joke is Russia has always been incompetent.McCain was actually on the money in 2008.
sentences.zh: 笑话是俄罗斯一直无能。麦凯恩2008年其实就靠钱了。

sentences.en: He was in the audience when Putin gave that famous "warning to the west" speech in Germany [1].The quip that Russia is a "gas station with nuclear weapons" about sums it up.
sentences.zh: 当普京在德国发表著名的“向西方发出警告”的演讲时，他就在观众席中[1]。“俄罗斯是一个拥有核武器的加油站”的俏皮话概括了这一点。

sentences.en: The United States being weak or not really has nothing to do with it.
sentences.zh: 美国弱不强其实跟这个没有什么关系。

sentences.en: They counted on Zelensky cutting and running, the attempted decapitation of Kyiv failed.
sentences.zh: 他们指望泽伦斯基逃跑，但基辅斩首行动却失败了。

sentences.en: Russia is swirling the toilet.
sentences.zh: 俄罗斯正在旋转马桶。

sentences.en: Without the energy reserves their oligarchs would be worthless.
sentences.zh: 如果没有能源储备，他们的寡头就毫无价值。

sentences.en: Since most of what is actually valuable about their country is land (where the resources sit) that is sparsely inhabited the end state of China controlling most of what is now Russia is inevitable.
sentences.zh: 由于他们国家真正有价值的大部分是人烟稀少的土地（资源所在），中国控制现在俄罗斯大部分地区的最终状态是不可避免的。

sentences.en: By failing in Europe and tying himself to China, Putin has ensured Russian power will continue to erode.
sentences.zh: 通过在欧洲的失败并将自己与中国联系在一起，普京确保了俄罗斯的实力将继续受到削弱。

sentences.en: Of course, it's not great for China, having to deal with two nuclear armed pariahs on their borders.The United States is truly blessed simply by being two oceans away from anyone who could remotely challenge it.
sentences.zh: 当然，这对中国来说并不是一件好事，因为它必须在边境上与两个拥有核武器的贱民打交道。美国确实很幸运，因为它与任何可以远程挑战它的人相距两个大洋。

sentences.en: Properly understood, the Monroe (or "Donroe" doctrine) is less about foreign intervention and more an understanding that if a peer power ever emerged in the Western Hemisphere, most of what appears to make the United States singular would rapidly disappear.[1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hQ58Yv6kP44
sentences.zh: 正确理解的话，门罗（或“唐罗”主义）与其说是关于外国干预，不如说是一种理解，即如果西半球出现了同等强国，那么大多数看似使美国独特的东西都会迅速消失。 [1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hQ58Yv6kP44

> Now they struggle against Iran

blocks.html_en:
Yes but everyone knows you can't eliminate the risk from modern MANPADs even with fancy electronic systems and flares. They are conducting combat flights over a country with thousands of chinese MANPADs, the fact they haven't lost more planes is really a great achievement.

The US lost 73 planes in Desert Storm to Iraq and that war only lasted 43 days. By comparison the losses from Iran have been shockingly low and shows how much US navy/airforce capabilities have matured.

sentences.en: Yes but everyone knows you can't eliminate the risk from modern MANPADs even with fancy electronic systems and flares.
sentences.zh: 是的，但每个人都知道，即​​使拥有先进的电子系统和照明弹，也无法消除现代便携式防空系统的风险。

sentences.en: They are conducting combat flights over a country with thousands of chinese MANPADs, the fact they haven't lost more planes is really a great achievement.The US lost 73 planes in Desert Storm to Iraq and that war only lasted 43 days.
sentences.zh: 他们在一个拥有数千架中国便携式防空系统的国家上空进行战斗飞行，他们没有损失更多的飞机真是一个伟大的成就。美国在伊拉克的沙漠风暴中损失了73架飞机，那场战争只持续了43天。

sentences.en: By comparison the losses from Iran have been shockingly low and shows how much US navy/airforce capabilities have matured.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，伊朗的损失低得惊人，这表明美国海军/空军的能力已经成熟了多少。

blocks.html_en:
MANPADS are mostly relevant to helicopters, pretty useless against a jet at altitudes. You need a SHORAD system at the minimum, like a Pantsir, and they have electronic signatures that make them targets.

Most of the aircraft losses in Desert Storm were due to CFIT and flying into AAA and low altitude weapons. This was a relic of cold war tactics for penetrating Warsaw Pact defences. Once they started flying higher the problem was contained.

See.e.g. https://balloonstodrones.com/2022/10/19/looking-back-at-iraq...

sentences.en: MANPADS are mostly relevant to helicopters, pretty useless against a jet at altitudes.
sentences.zh: 便携式防空系统主要与直升机相关，但对于高空的喷气式飞机几乎毫无用处。

sentences.en: You need a SHORAD system at the minimum, like a Pantsir, and they have electronic signatures that make them targets.Most of the aircraft losses in Desert Storm were due to CFIT and flying into AAA and low altitude weapons.
sentences.zh: 你至少需要一个 SHORAD 系统，比如 Pantsir，它们有电子签名，可以使它们成为目标。沙漠风暴中的大部分飞机损失都是由于 CFIT 以及飞入 AAA 和低空武器造成的。

sentences.en: This was a relic of cold war tactics for penetrating Warsaw Pact defences.
sentences.zh: 这是冷战时期用于突破华沙条约组织防御的战术的遗迹。

sentences.en: Once they started flying higher the problem was contained.See.e.g. https://balloonstodrones.com/2022/10/19/looking-back-at-iraq...
sentences.zh: 一旦他们开始飞得更高，问题就得到了遏制。参见例如： https://balloonstodrones.com/2022/10/19/looking-back-at-iraq...

blocks.html_en: Fair enough. I read the F-15E that went down was likely a MANPAD because it was flying low at the time and there were spotters. Or it was some clever SHORAD ambush with something they kept hidden.

sentences.zh: 很公平。

sentences.en: Or it was some clever SHORAD ambush with something they kept hidden.
sentences.zh: 或者这是一场巧妙的肖拉德伏击，他们隐藏了一些东西。

blocks.html_en:
Using F-15s/A-10s to hunt for Shaheed launchers is inherently susceptible to ambush. They are doctrinally driven to attack the launch teams because of their theory of victory, when intercepting vehicles in flight is much safer.

If the USAF had combat capable drones, for ISR, ground strike and intercept, that wouldn't be such an issue. But their ISR drones are too slow and expensive, same with Reaper.

sentences.en: Using F-15s/A-10s to hunt for Shaheed launchers is inherently susceptible to ambush.
sentences.zh: 使用 F-15/A-10 寻找“沙希德”发射器本质上很容易遭到伏击。

sentences.en: They are doctrinally driven to attack the launch teams because of their theory of victory, when intercepting vehicles in flight is much safer.If the USAF had combat capable drones, for ISR, ground strike and intercept, that wouldn't be such an issue.
sentences.zh: 他们在理论上会攻击发射小组，因为他们的胜利理论认为，拦截飞行中的车辆要安全得多。如果美国空军拥有具有战斗能力的无人机，用于情监侦、地面打击和拦截，那就不会是这样的问题。

sentences.en: But their ISR drones are too slow and expensive, same with Reaper.
sentences.zh: 但他们的 ISR 无人机速度太慢且昂贵，与“死神”无人机一样。

blocks.html_en:
> In the first Iraq war, they went in and won. In the second one, it took years to sort of occupy the country.

The objectives for the two wars were incredibly different, making this an apples to oranges comparison. In the first war, the objective was to push Iraqi troops out of Kuwait (achieved) and then to contain the Iraqi military within Iraq (achieved). In the second war, the objectives were much broader and much harder to achieve. They achieved some objectives (toppling the existing government, defeating the existing military), and then utterly failed in nation building and got into a protracted conflict with state and non-state sponsored military groups.

sentences.en: > In the first Iraq war, they went in and won.
sentences.zh: > 在第一次伊拉克战争中，他们参战并取得了胜利。

sentences.en: In the second one, it took years to sort of occupy the country.The objectives for the two wars were incredibly different, making this an apples to oranges comparison.
sentences.zh: 在第二场战争中，花了数年时间才占领了这个国家。两次战争的目标截然不同，这使得这是一个苹果与橙子的比较。

sentences.en: In the first war, the objective was to push Iraqi troops out of Kuwait (achieved) and then to contain the Iraqi military within Iraq (achieved).
sentences.zh: 在第一次战争中，目标是将伊拉克军队赶出科威特（已实现），然后将伊拉克军队遏制在伊拉克境内（已实现）。

sentences.en: In the second war, the objectives were much broader and much harder to achieve.
sentences.zh: 在第二次战争中，目标更加广泛，实现起来也更加困难。

sentences.en: They achieved some objectives (toppling the existing government, defeating the existing military), and then utterly failed in nation building and got into a protracted conflict with state and non-state sponsored military groups.
sentences.zh: 他们实现了一些目标（推翻现有政府，击败现有军队），然后在国家建设方面彻底失败，并与国家和非国家支持的军事团体陷入旷日持久的冲突。

blocks.html_en: Some nation building occurred, but mostly corruption. This was after the first war where they deliberately destroyed civilian infrastructure like power plants, water treatment plants, hospitals and such to create a failed nation. A country that was one of the most modern and developed middle eastern countries before the war.

blocks.html_en: Because the most powerful military in the world can only do so much when ordered to execute a crap strategy by civilian leadership. Desert Storm worked because it followed the Powell Doctrine and was bounded to ejecting Iraq from Kuwait. Iraqi Freedom muddled through incoherently because the Bush and Obama administrations didn't want to understand the actual problem set they were faced with, but ultimately ended up with a reasonably stable country after way too much effort and time. Iran is what happens when you have an administration who thinks you can win a war by "just bombing things," which is ironically the stereotype most of the internet has about any kind of military action.

sentences.en: Because the most powerful military in the world can only do so much when ordered to execute a crap strategy by civilian leadership.
sentences.zh: 因为世界上最强大的军队在文官领导下令执行垃圾战略时只能做这么多。

sentences.en: Desert Storm worked because it followed the Powell Doctrine and was bounded to ejecting Iraq from Kuwait.
sentences.zh: 沙漠风暴之所以有效，是因为它遵循鲍威尔主义，注定要把伊拉克赶出科威特。

sentences.en: Iraqi Freedom muddled through incoherently because the Bush and Obama administrations didn't want to understand the actual problem set they were faced with, but ultimately ended up with a reasonably stable country after way too much effort and time.
sentences.zh: 由于布什和奥巴马政府不想了解他们面临的实际问题，“伊拉克自由”的进程语无伦次，但经过太多的努力和时间，最终还是建立了一个相当稳定的国家。

sentences.en: Iran is what happens when you have an administration who thinks you can win a war by "just bombing things," which is ironically the stereotype most of the internet has about any kind of military action.
sentences.zh: 当你的政府认为你可以通过“轰炸”来赢得战争时，就会发生伊朗问题，讽刺的是，这正是大多数互联网上对任何类型的军事行动的刻板印象。

Did it? You mean operational govt fell. The country was fought over for 10 yrs, which the U.S. then backed out.

Israel got a regime change while U.S. tax payers received the bill.

In the end, it’s still a country.

sentences.en: You mean operational govt fell.
sentences.zh: 你的意思是运营政府垮台了。

sentences.en: The country was fought over for 10 yrs, which the U.S. then backed out.Israel got a regime change while U.S. tax payers received the bill.In the end, it’s still a country.

blocks.html_en: I'm not saying that the war didn't go on for 10 years, it did. Baathist government fell and the US led Coalition Provisional Authority took over running the country. You have US troops in the whole country and US control of all civil institutions. The only way that's not "occupying" the country is if you redefine what the word mean. With your definition France, Norway, Denmark, Poland, and many more were not occupied during ww2.

sentences.en: I'm not saying that the war didn't go on for 10 years, it did.
sentences.zh: 我并不是说战争没有持续十年，它确实持续了十年。

sentences.en: Baathist government fell and the US led Coalition Provisional Authority took over running the country.
sentences.zh: 复兴党政府垮台，美国领导的联军临时政府接管了国家的管理。

sentences.en: You have US troops in the whole country and US control of all civil institutions.
sentences.zh: 美军遍布全国，美国控制着所有民事机构。

sentences.en: The only way that's not "occupying" the country is if you redefine what the word mean.
sentences.zh: 不“占领”这个国家的唯一方法是重新定义这个词的含义。

sentences.en: With your definition France, Norway, Denmark, Poland, and many more were not occupied during ww2.
sentences.zh: 根据你的定义，法国、挪威、丹麦、波兰等许多国家在二战期间没有被占领。

blocks.html_en:
As I understand it, they done this in simulation, and the human pilots did not fare well.

I don't know of the computer aircraft were able to fly beyond human limits (i.e. they don't black out) or not. If the computers can actually fly to what the airframe is capable of, they would have a distinct advantage over the human pilots.

sentences.en: As I understand it, they done this in simulation, and the human pilots did not fare well.I don't know of the computer aircraft were able to fly beyond human limits (i.e. they don't black out) or not.
sentences.zh: 据我了解，他们是在模拟中做到这一点的，而人类飞行员的表现并不好。我不知道计算机飞机是否能够飞出人类的极限（即它们不会停电）。

sentences.en: If the computers can actually fly to what the airframe is capable of, they would have a distinct advantage over the human pilots.
sentences.zh: 如果计算机实际上能够达到机身的飞行能力，那么它们将比人类飞行员具有明显的优势。

blocks.html_en:
I think they did it in simulation with several.

In any case, dogfighting is commonly done because it's great training but in most cases, you expect most Air to Air combat being flinging missiles at each other.

sentences.en: I think they did it in simulation with several.In any case, dogfighting is commonly done because it's great training but in most cases, you expect most Air to Air combat being flinging missiles at each other.
sentences.zh: 我认为他们是在模拟中做到了这一点。无论如何，缠斗是很常见的，因为这是很好的训练，但在大多数情况下，你预计大多数空对空战斗都是互相投掷导弹。

blocks.html_en:
The first time an airline pilot flies a new aircraft type in the real world they fly it with a plane full of passengers. All the training up to that point is done in a simulator.

Now that first flight is with an experienced training captain but the point is the simulator is good enough that the FAA has signed off on using that as the primary training device.

It's been this way for at least 25 years.

sentences.en: The first time an airline pilot flies a new aircraft type in the real world they fly it with a plane full of passengers.
sentences.zh: 航空公司飞行员第一次在现实世界中驾驶一种新机型时，他们会驾驶一架满载乘客的飞机。

sentences.en: All the training up to that point is done in a simulator.Now that first flight is with an experienced training captain but the point is the simulator is good enough that the FAA has signed off on using that as the primary training device.It's been this way for at least 25 years.
sentences.zh: 到那时为止的所有训练都是在模拟器中完成的。现在第一次飞行是由一位经验丰富的训练机长进行的，但重点是模拟器足够好，以至于 FAA 已经批准使用它作为主要训练设备。这种方式已经至少 25 年了。

Now that first flight is with an experienced training captain but the point is the simulator is good enough that the FAA has signed off on using that as the primary training device.

It's been this way for at least 25 years.

sentences.en: The first time an airline pilot flies a new aircraft type in the real world they fly it with a plane full of passengers.
sentences.zh: 航空公司飞行员第一次在现实世界中驾驶一种新机型时，他们会驾驶一架满载乘客的飞机。

sentences.en: All the training up to that point is done in a simulator.Now that first flight is with an experienced training captain but the point is the simulator is good enough that the FAA has signed off on using that as the primary training device.It's been this way for at least 25 years.
sentences.zh: 到那时为止的所有训练都是在模拟器中完成的。现在第一次飞行是由一位经验丰富的训练机长进行的，但重点是模拟器足够好，以至于 FAA 已经批准使用它作为主要训练设备。这种方式已经至少 25 年了。

sentences.zh: 现在，第一次飞行是由一位经验丰富的训练机长进行的，但关键是模拟器足够好，美国联邦航空局已经批准使用它作为主要训练设备。

blocks.html_en:
Many of the crash reports from the likes of Mentor Pilot on YouTube call out insufficient type-experience as a cause of accidents. Sometimes it's "the same" type, but the manufacturers manipulated the situation, the changes mean unfamiliarity and that leads to deaths.

Not giving pilots live training on new types is for financial reasons. It doesn't cause more deaths than the cost differential between training and compensation allow for.

sentences.en: Many of the crash reports from the likes of Mentor Pilot on YouTube call out insufficient type-experience as a cause of accidents.
sentences.zh: YouTube 上 Mentor Pilot 等网站的许多事故报告都指出，类型经验不足是事故发生的原因之一。

sentences.en: Sometimes it's "the same" type, but the manufacturers manipulated the situation, the changes mean unfamiliarity and that leads to deaths.Not giving pilots live training on new types is for financial reasons.
sentences.zh: 有时是“相同”类型，但制造商操纵了情况，这些变化意味着不熟悉并导致死亡。不给飞行员提供新类型的现场培训是出于经济原因。

sentences.en: It doesn't cause more deaths than the cost differential between training and compensation allow for.
sentences.zh: 它造成的死亡人数不会超过培训和补偿之间的成本差异。

human pilots have physiological constraints that AI doesnt.

onboard computers are not immune to G's but have a substantially higher threshold until physical mechanisms fail due to strain.

blocks.html_en: You don't need F-16s at all if you have cheap drones. Why send an expensive plane with a pilot to bomb some country if you can send a drone that costs as little as a basic SUV instead?

blocks.html_en:
And yet the Russian war machine pushes on.

Opening up ways to pressure the Russian economy in a war of choice does equate to achieving decisive battlefield advantages.

There's a reason those drones hit oil refineries and not steel works.

That's not to undermine how the strategic environment has changed, but to imagine that now - after the change - it'll obviously stay that way forever is ridiculous.

Ukraine is not being destroyed by unstoppable swarms of cheap drones which Russia is perfectly capable of producing. So clearly this story is more complicated.

sentences.en: And yet the Russian war machine pushes on.Opening up ways to pressure the Russian economy in a war of choice does equate to achieving decisive battlefield advantages.There's a reason those drones hit oil refineries and not steel works.That's not to undermine how the strategic environment has changed, but to imagine that now - after the change - it'll obviously stay that way forever is ridiculous.Ukraine is not being destroyed by unstoppable swarms of cheap drones which Russia is perfectly capable of producing.
sentences.zh: 然而俄罗斯的战争机器仍在继续前进。在一场选择的战争中开辟对俄罗斯经济施压的方式确实等同于获得决定性的战场优势。这些无人机袭击炼油厂而不是钢铁厂是有原因的。这并不是要破坏战略环境已经发生的变化，而是想象现在——变化之后——它显然会永远保持这种状态是荒谬的。乌克兰并没有被俄罗斯完全有能力生产的大量廉价无人机所摧毁。

sentences.en: So clearly this story is more complicated.
sentences.zh: 显然这个故事要复杂得多。

blocks.html_en:
>And yet the Russian war machine pushes on

Mostly on foot now. They tried using tanks today in Dobropillia, which was very rare and surprising! 28 (https://nitter.tiekoetter.com/NotWoofers/status/208005697904... didnt even know they had that many left and concentrated in one spot) destroyed in an ~hour, attack routed.

>Ukraine is not being destroyed by unstoppable swarms of cheap drones which Russia is perfectly capable of producing.

"At least 577 civilians were killed by short-range drones in Ukraine in 2025" https://www.france24.com/en/tv-shows/the-observers/20260221-...

They arent unstoppable to Ukrainians _now_. Even mighty US just gave up its bases letting Iran destroy hundreds of millions of infrastructure (and kill base staff) with zero attempts at defense. Afaik only Baghdad base has a C-Ram that actually works against flying lawnmovers.

Yes, its more complicated, horses for courses etc. But the idea one needs a jet or cruise missiles to bomb an adversary is an outdated one and whoever clings to it will pay dearly. Its probably a matter of time before Iran tries mass drones launched off a container ship into continental US with zero resistance.

sentences.en: >And yet the Russian war machine pushes onMostly on foot now.
sentences.zh: >然而俄罗斯战争机器现在主要是步行前进。

sentences.en: They tried using tanks today in Dobropillia, which was very rare and surprising!
sentences.zh: 他们今天在多布罗皮利亚尝试使用坦克，这是非常罕见和令人惊讶的！

sentences.en: 28 (https://nitter.tiekoetter.com/NotWoofers/status/208005697904... didnt even know they had that many left and concentrated in one spot) destroyed in an ~hour, attack routed.>Ukraine is not being destroyed by unstoppable swarms of cheap drones which Russia is perfectly capable of producing."At least 577 civilians were killed by short-range drones in Ukraine in 2025" https://www.france24.com/en/tv-shows/the-observers/20260221-...They arent unstoppable to Ukrainians _now_.
sentences.zh: 28架飞机（https://nitter.tiekoetter.com/NotWoofers/status/208005697904...甚至不知道他们还剩下那么多飞机并集中在一个地方）在一小时内被摧毁，攻击被击溃。>乌克兰并没有被俄罗斯完全有能力生产的大量廉价无人机所摧毁。“乌克兰至少有 577 名平民被短程无人机杀死。 2025” https://www.france24.com/en/tv-shows/the-observers/20260221-...他们对乌克兰人_现在_来说并不是不可阻挡的。

sentences.en: Even mighty US just gave up its bases letting Iran destroy hundreds of millions of infrastructure (and kill base staff) with zero attempts at defense.

blocks.html_en:
>And yet the Russian war machine pushes on

Mostly on foot now. They tried using tanks today in Dobropillia, which was very rare and surprising! 28 (https://nitter.tiekoetter.com/NotWoofers/status/208005697904... didnt even know they had that many left and concentrated in one spot) destroyed in an ~hour, attack routed.

>Ukraine is not being destroyed by unstoppable swarms of cheap drones which Russia is perfectly capable of producing.

"At least 577 civilians were killed by short-range drones in Ukraine in 2025" https://www.france24.com/en/tv-shows/the-observers/20260221-...

They arent unstoppable to Ukrainians _now_. Even mighty US just gave up its bases letting Iran destroy hundreds of millions of infrastructure (and kill base staff) with zero attempts at defense. Afaik only Baghdad base has a C-Ram that actually works against flying lawnmovers.

Yes, its more complicated, horses for courses etc. But the idea one needs a jet or cruise missiles to bomb an adversary is an outdated one and whoever clings to it will pay dearly. Its probably a matter of time before Iran tries mass drones launched off a container ship into continental US with zero resistance.

blocks.html_en:
Human control in flight has often taken a backseat to automation. For example, the SR-71, built in the 60s flew too fast to rely on human decision making for several tasks. If they were off by a fraction of a second, they would miss their intended recon target; so, both the navigation and sensor control were largely automated.

Seems odd to invest this much into AI flight of aircraft designed for manned flight, when it would be better to build craft spec'd for AI flight without human life support systems.

sentences.en: Human control in flight has often taken a backseat to automation.
sentences.zh: 飞行中的人工控制常常让位于自动化。

sentences.en: For example, the SR-71, built in the 60s flew too fast to rely on human decision making for several tasks.
sentences.zh: 例如，20 世纪 60 年代制造的 SR-71 飞行速度太快，无法依靠人类决策来完成多项任务。

sentences.en: If they were off by a fraction of a second, they would miss their intended recon target; so, both the navigation and sensor control were largely automated.Seems odd to invest this much into AI flight of aircraft designed for manned flight, when it would be better to build craft spec'd for AI flight without human life support systems.
sentences.zh: 如果他们偏离了几分之一秒，他们就会错过预定的侦察目标；因此，导航和传感器控制在很大程度上都是自动化的。在专为载人飞行而设计的飞机的人工智能飞行上投入如此多的资金似乎很奇怪，而在没有人类生命支持系统的情况下建造专门用于人工智能飞行的飞行器会更好。

blocks.html_en:
The US military has thousands of old airframes, many of which can be made flyable with very short turnaround. They already use the F-16 as test targets.

Taking an obsolete airframe that is already paid for and adding AI to it is much cheaper than building a completely new AI-native airframe from scratch. This is finding a new use for old aircraft. You can send a bunch of old AI-driven F-16s on high-risk missions without worrying about a human pilot or cost. The airframes may be relatively obsolete but can still do plenty of damage on their day.

The new 6th generation aircraft are all being designed as "manned optional". That is probably the last generation with affordances for a pilot.

sentences.en: The US military has thousands of old airframes, many of which can be made flyable with very short turnaround.
sentences.zh: 美国军方拥有数千架旧机身，其中许多可以在很短的周转时间内实现飞行。

sentences.en: They already use the F-16 as test targets.Taking an obsolete airframe that is already paid for and adding AI to it is much cheaper than building a completely new AI-native airframe from scratch.
sentences.zh: 他们已经使用 F-16 作为测试目标。采用已经付费的过时机身并向其添加 AI 比从头开始构建全新的 AI 原生机身便宜得多。

sentences.en: This is finding a new use for old aircraft.
sentences.zh: 这正在为旧飞机找到新用途。

sentences.zh: 您可以派出一堆旧式人工智能驱动的 F-16 来执行高风险任务，而无需担心人类飞行员或成本。

sentences.en: The airframes may be relatively obsolete but can still do plenty of damage on their day.The new 6th generation aircraft are all being designed as "manned optional".
sentences.zh: 这些机身可能相对过时，但在使用时仍然会造成大量损坏。新的第六代飞机都被设计为“载人可选”。

sentences.en: That is probably the last generation with affordances for a pilot.
sentences.zh: 这可能是最后一代有能力担任飞行员的人。

blocks.html_en: The F16 was the first fully fly-by-wire fighter jet. It's an ideal platform for AI experiments. Having AI "wing men" can be a great tactical advantage, and they're expendable.

sentences.zh: F16 是第一架完全电传操纵的战斗机。

sentences.en: It's an ideal platform for AI experiments.
sentences.zh: 它是人工智能实验的理想平台。

sentences.en: Having AI "wing men" can be a great tactical advantage, and they're expendable.

blocks.html_en: The way I see it, this is the JDAM of drones. Will it perform better than purpose built aircraft? No. But the USAF has a few hundred old F-16s entering retirement that would be mothballed otherwise, and strapping some cheap sensors to them is a fast way to play around with the idea while they wait for Lockheed and Anduril to finish the good stuff.

sentences.en: The way I see it, this is the JDAM of drones.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，这就是无人机界的 JDAM。

sentences.en: Will it perform better than purpose built aircraft?
sentences.zh: 它的性能会比专用飞机更好吗？

sentences.en: No. But the USAF has a few hundred old F-16s entering retirement that would be mothballed otherwise, and strapping some cheap sensors to them is a fast way to play around with the idea while they wait for Lockheed and Anduril to finish the good stuff.
sentences.zh: 不会。但美国空军有数百架旧 F-16 即将退役，否则它们将被封存，在等待洛克希德和安杜里尔完成好东西时，在它们身上绑上一些廉价传感器是一种快速实现这个想法的方法。

No. But the USAF has a few hundred old F-16s entering retirement that would be mothballed otherwise, and strapping some cheap sensors to them is a fast way to play around with the idea while they wait for Lockheed and Anduril to finish the good stuff.

sentences.zh: 在我看来，这就是无人机界的 JDAM。

sentences.en: Will it perform better than purpose built aircraft?
sentences.zh: 它的性能会比专用飞机更好吗？

sentences.en: No. But the USAF has a few hundred old F-16s entering retirement that would be mothballed otherwise, and strapping some cheap sensors to them is a fast way to play around with the idea while they wait for Lockheed and Anduril to finish the good stuff.
sentences.zh: 不会。但美国空军有数百架旧 F-16 即将退役，否则它们将被封存，在等待洛克希德和安杜里尔完成好东西时，在它们身上绑上一些廉价传感器是一种快速实现这个想法的方法。

blocks.html_en: The Right Stuff by Tom Wolfe has a lot of interesting stuff on this wrt the first astronauts (who were sourced from test pilots). The test pilots saw the astronauts as second class citizens, at first, because they weren't actually doing any steering. Once the astronauts became famous, they started demanding more manual takeovers, which the engineers didn't want to build because it could only really hurt, but the engineers were forced to build them!

With apologies, I don't particularly enjoy when people do this, and I don't think it adds a lot, but I want to leave this breadcrumb, I wrote a post on this a couple days ago here, on an anti-AI screed submission, saying this anti-politic, this accountability denial, is very much the point.

Taking this quote and seeing exactly that potential for abuse seems very very much like the point. Strong agree, strongly against leaving non-seeing non-thinking alien intelligences with no accountability and proprietary programming to judge us. https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48965083

blocks.html_en: Boeing 737 MAX often (and semi-regularly) suicides itself and also kills many people in the process. So now fighter jets are AI controlled, by one government (or, any other government, by the way; that problem is not confined to the USA only). I am sorry but this also means that relying on such fighter jets becomes a problem for other countries depending on that. Essentially another country controls here what other people can do here. Of course objectively this only becomes a problem when there is a war between the country that purchased those fighter jets and wants to fight the country that sold the fighter jet (as otherwise it would hardly matter), but imagine how much blackmail-pressure other countries get into as a dependency here. You can just replace the USA with China here and suddenly it may be easier to see the problem. Considering the huge cost of those fighter jets it seems as if the media deliberately avoids wanting to talk about those problems. But we know of those problems already in other areas - see the right to repair movement, which in turn is a right to ownership movement. Who really owns fighter jets?

sentences.en: Boeing 737 MAX often (and semi-regularly) suicides itself and also kills many people in the process.
sentences.zh: 波音 737 MAX 经常（半定期）自杀，并在此过程中造成许多人死亡。

sentences.en: So now fighter jets are AI controlled, by one government (or, any other government, by the way; that problem is not confined to the USA only).
sentences.zh: 因此，现在战斗机是由人工智能控制的，由一个政府（或任何其他政府，顺便说一句；这个问题不仅限于美国）。

sentences.en: I am sorry but this also means that relying on such fighter jets becomes a problem for other countries depending on that.
sentences.zh: 我很抱歉，但这也意味着依赖这种战斗机对其他依赖于此的国家来说是一个问题。

sentences.en: Essentially another country controls here what other people can do here.
sentences.zh: 本质上，另一个国家在这里控制着其他人在这里可以做的事情。

sentences.en: Of course objectively this only becomes a problem when there is a war between the country that purchased those fighter jets and wants to fight the country that sold the fighter jet (as otherwise it would hardly matter), but imagine how much blackmail-pressure other countries get into as a dependency here.
sentences.zh: 当然，客观上，只有当购买这些战斗机的国家想要与出售战斗机的国家作战时，这才会成为一个问题（否则就没什么关系了），但想象一下其他国家作为依赖国会承受多大的勒索压力。

sentences.en: You can just replace the USA with China here and suddenly it may be easier to see the problem.
sentences.zh: 你可以在这里用中国代替美国，突然间可能会更容易看到问题。

sentences.en: Considering the huge cost of those fighter jets it seems as if the media deliberately avoids wanting to talk about those problems.
sentences.zh: 考虑到这些战斗机的巨大成本，媒体似乎故意避免谈论这些问题。

sentences.en: But we know of those problems already in other areas - see the right to repair movement, which in turn is a right to ownership movement.
sentences.zh: 但我们知道其他领域已经存在这些问题——参见修复权运动，这反过来又是所有权运动。

sentences.en: Who really owns fighter jets?
sentences.zh: 谁真正拥有战斗机？

blocks.html_en:
Autonomous driving is still nowhere near 100%, so why suddenly jump to fighter jets? Is this more of Hegseth's foolhardy bluster and injected testosterone? The tech isn't ready. They should start with golf-karts, not fighter planes.

-----------

Edit: Okay gang. If aircraft are easier to fly than cars are to drive, where are all your pilot licenses? If you've faced rush hour traffic, flying a military jet should be a breeze! Please, go volunteer your neighbourhoods to be the first for AI jets to be tested over. We can put a mock enemy target in an empty lot and watch them pick it off, first try, with zero collateral damage. Easy peasy!

sentences.en: Autonomous driving is still nowhere near 100%, so why suddenly jump to fighter jets?
sentences.zh: 自动驾驶还远未达到100%，那么为什么突然跳到战斗机呢？

sentences.en: Is this more of Hegseth's foolhardy bluster and injected testosterone?
sentences.zh: 这更像是赫格斯的鲁莽咆哮和注射睾酮吗？

sentences.en: The tech isn't ready.
sentences.zh: 技术还没准备好。

sentences.en: They should start with golf-karts, not fighter planes.-----------Edit: Okay gang.
sentences.zh: 他们应该从高尔夫卡丁车开始，而不是战斗机。------------编辑：好的，伙计。

sentences.en: If aircraft are easier to fly than cars are to drive, where are all your pilot licenses?
sentences.zh: 如果驾驶飞机比驾驶汽车更容易，那么您的飞行员执照在哪里？

sentences.en: If you've faced rush hour traffic, flying a military jet should be a breeze!

Autonomous driving is still nowhere near 100%, so why suddenly jump to fighter jets? Is this more of Hegseth's foolhardy bluster and injected testosterone? The tech isn't ready. They should start with golf-karts, not fighter planes.

blocks.html_en: Fighter jets don't need 100%. In a war, we accept a much higher rate of casualties than we do in day to day driving. blocks.html_en: As another poster already said, building new F-16s would be a waste of resources, but using the old existing F-16s by automating them is likely to be a better use of the existing resources, instead of scraping them. This also allows to gain experience that might be useful for designing the future unmanned airframes.

sentences.en: As another poster already said, building new F-16s would be a waste of resources, but using the old existing F-16s by automating them is likely to be a better use of the existing resources, instead of scraping them.
sentences.zh: 正如另一位发帖者已经说过的那样，建造新的 F-16 会浪费资源，但通过自动化来使用现有的旧 F-16 可能会更好地利用现有资源，而不是浪费它们。

sentences.en: This also allows to gain experience that might be useful for designing the future unmanned airframes.
sentences.zh: 这还可以获得对设计未来无人机机身可能有用的经验。

content_html_zh: 对我来说最疯狂的事情之一是，F-16 是专门为人类更容易地在高重力下执行空对空战斗角色而设计的。例如，飞行杆位于右侧，而不是在飞行员的两腿之间（像大多数飞机一样）。 这是 b/c:a。 G 服的膨胀可能会影响飞行员握住操纵杆的能力（这就是为什么蓝天使飞行时会穿着“剪裁”的 G 服，顺便说一句，他们驾驶的是 F-18）b。 摇杆是“向左轻推”而不是“向左按住”。 例如要开始转弯，请将操纵杆向左轻推，然后让它返回零，要结束转弯，请向右轻推操纵杆。 在普通飞机上，转弯时将其握在左侧。 同样，这是为了减少飞行员在高 G 时的努力> 该套件采用了与飞机飞行控制和任务系统的新颖接口，允许飞行员通过轻按开关在传统人类控制和人工智能控制之间切换。 这确保了人类在环实验的安全、可靠的环境。究竟如何确保安全可靠的环境？ 当自动系统达到极限并将问题抛给人类时，人类非常不擅长突然接管。 当自动驾驶仪脱离并将配置不良的飞机的控制权交还给人类飞行员时，许多空中混乱和坠机事件就会开始。无论如何，计算机一直在“驾驶”F-16。 这是一架静态不稳定的飞机。我想知道人工智能控制是否向与人类飞行员相同的飞行控制系统提供输入，或者它是否有一个可以绕过人类操作系统中的控制法则的较低级别接口。一方面，你可能对人工智能想出正常控制法则不允许的飞行方法感兴趣，但另一方面，你不希望人工智能想出令人兴奋的新方法来偏离受控飞行（可能处于不可恢复的姿态）飞行员驾驶非常昂贵的飞机（即使飞行员可以弹射，这是一项充满生命和肢体风险的努力）。编辑：大概因为飞机上有飞行员，人工智能必须至少受到一些常规控制法的约束，这些规则阻止飞行员进行从重力加载角度来看与人类生命不相容的动作。至少有人会希望。我不认为我会和一个能够以可能杀死我的方式驾驶飞机的虚拟飞行员一起飞行，但是否则请保持飞机完好无损。好吧，意外地产生与人类生命不相容的重力负载可能会给人工智能带来以前被剥夺的自由。 毕竟，机器人三大定律只是科幻小说。> 决定移除其人工操作员，而不是等待攻击的最终批准。这当然是明智的一种方法。 遗憾的是并没有那么令人惊讶。> 无论如何，计算机始终在“驾驶”F-16。 这是一架静态不稳定的飞机。呃... 我... 你是认真的？ 当然，从某种意义上说，人工智能是一台计算机，补偿固有飞行不稳定性的奇特状态机也是一台计算机。 但将两者等同起来是荒谬的。关键是，众所周知，F-16 上的所有控制输入都是电子的（不是数字的，但在最早的型号上仍然是电子的），因此与其他一些使用部分辅助机械控制的飞机（如 A-10A 或 F-15E 或其他飞机）相比，对其进行完全计算机控制“更容易”。我不认为他们把它等同起来，更重要的是，从技术上来说，它已经是一台总是在驾驶飞机的计算机，但人工智能和飞行控制系统是完全不同的。这是一个公平的评论，因为他们刚刚讨论了人工智能如何控制飞机——它会向状态机提供输入还是直接控制所有飞行系统？ 人们强烈地假设输入必须是状态机的。 ..但是天哪，将完整的系统控制权交给它不是很有趣吗！呃…… 它是一台计算机，就像...... 有些软件功能可以比人类更快地完成定义明确、有限的事情。 但暗示 f16 飞行软件（顺便说一句，这是古老的）与这个“人工智能”类似，充其量只是一种误导。我不认为这是对你所回应的评论的一个很好的解读。 该评论实际上只是说控制回路中已经有一台计算机，并且该计算机负责稳定性所需的非常快的响应。 这意味着人工智能不需要复制这些功能。抱歉，我的“等同”对你来说太冒犯了。我发表评论的原因更多的是思考人工智能如何与数字飞行控制计算机交互（即它的输入是否像人类一样由软件介导）。如果人工智能是一个神经网络，那么从某种意义上说，它也只是一个奇特的查找表。看看波音 737 MAX 就知道了。 这是不稳定飞机的最好例子。 当然，人工智能也是计算机。 这些比较都不是“荒谬的”。MAX 只是有一个“鸭翼”，危险地向前突出，在高阿尔法时产生上仰力矩。 与 F-16 非常不同，F-16 的整个 CoL 太靠前。> 看看波音 737 MAX 就知道了。 这是不稳定飞机的最好例子。不。不是空气动力学意义上的。波音 737 MAX 很稳定。 从这个意义上说：“术语稳定性是指飞机在受到干扰后保持或恢复到经过调整的飞行状态的趋势。” 这意味着，如果有一阵微风吹过飞机，而飞行员没有把手放在控制装置上，飞机将返回原来的飞行路线，或保持新的飞行路线，而不是机腹朝上，然后坠毁在天空中。不稳定飞机的例子（空气动力学意义上的）：F-16、F-22、B-2 Spirit等。如果一阵风扰乱飞机，飞机需要调整操纵面以免坠落。从天而降。这些将是经验丰富的试飞员，他们非常擅长主动管理自动驾驶仪和其他自动化系统。 另外，它们还将得到地面控制器遥测的支持。 但最终军方愿意接受一些损失，以推进关键的研发项目。按照指定的 1.000 个目标/小时，五角大楼的“软件抽象层”（Maven 智能系统）分配 μ=60ms 来考虑添加到“杀伤链”中的每个（大规模）杀戮。对于米纳布女子学校的“双击”罢工，不可能有任何“管理层”考虑到这一点：1。 已在 Google 地图上出现超过 15 年2。 在 Google 搜索中排名第一。 即使在卫星图像上也是全粉红色的 确定性/模糊性旋钮如何更多地被部署来产生媒体热议，以获得短期政治利益，而不是战术或战略结果。（https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/mar/26/ai-got-the-blam...）《终结者》的错误在于，每个机器人都需要一个人在出现问题时承担法律责任。我对此表示怀疑。 问问自己：为什么 RQ-4 全球鹰或 MQ-9 死神需要这么多训练才能操作？ 操作员都是高薪、高技能的官员。这是一个关于人类“安全驾驶员”充当自动驾驶汽车的合法缓冲区的笑话。事实并非如此，飞行这些飞机可能非常危险，有很多无法恢复的情况。 但美国空军研究实验室 (AFRL)/美国宇航局 (NASA) 数十年来一直致力于此。 更高级别人工智能请求的机动由高度可靠的动力系统解算器使用模型预测控制（如自动驾驶仪）来执行，将飞机保持在安全范围内。该系统中的未知数是（a）飞行控制器的攻击性如何？ (b) 自治有多聪明？现代战斗机的飞行控制系统使得人为导致的偏离受控飞行变得困难。 这就是该系统的要点。几年前在麻省理工学院录制了一段精彩的 F-22 飞行员谈话，您可能会喜欢。 飞行员将 F-22 描述为他驾驶过的最简单的飞机，因为飞行控制系统处理了许多在低技术飞机中必须注意的注意事项（机身压力、避免可能偏离的姿态等）。 他谈到将非飞行员放入猛禽与 172 相比会更舒服。编辑：我相信这就是谈话：https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/16-687-private-pilot-ground-scho...不过，驾驶普通 F-16 绝对有可能逃离安全飞行包线。 F-22/F-35 你可以在这里用中国代替美国，突然间可能会更容易看到问题。 考虑到这些战斗机的巨大成本，媒体似乎故意避免谈论这些问题。 但我们知道其他领域已经存在这些问题——参见修复权运动，这反过来又是所有权运动。
